news.Title: The Dividend ETF Quietly Outperforming the S&P 500
news.Content:
The iShares Core High Dividend ETF (NYSEMKT: HDV) isn't as large or as popular as other dividend ETFs. As a result, many investors have likely missed that the fund is outperforming the S&P 500 this year. While that broad market index was up a strong 9% through the first half of the year, HDV is up more than 15%.

Here's a closer look at this top ETF and what has driven its quiet outperformance so far this year.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue В»

High-quality, high-yielding dividend stocks

The iShares Core High Dividend ETF is a passively managed fund that tracks an index of high-yielding U.S. dividend stocks ( Morningstar Dividend Yield Focus Index) . That index doesn't just screen companies based on their dividend yield. It also applies additional financial quality screens, including having a wide economic moat (sustainable competitive advantage) and a high distance to default (strong financial health). The index selects the highest-yielding stocks from those that pass its quality screens. It weights stocks in proportion to the dividends they pay.

The net result is an ETF that currently holds 75 stocks. The iShares Core High Dividend ETF has a 2.9% dividend yield based on its current price and dividend payments over the last 12 months. That's nearly three times higher than the S&P 500's current dividend yield of 1.1%.

The ETF's top five holdings are:

ExxonMobil : 7.1% weighing in the ETF.

AbbVie (NYSE: ABBV): 6.6%

Chevron : 5.4%

Verizon : 5.1%

Home Depot : 4.9%

While two of its top three holdings are energy stocks , the fund's overall allocation to the energy sector is 12.2% (third-largest sector). Consumer staples 24.5% and healthcare (23.9%) are its largest sectors.

What's driving the strong performance in 2026?

The fund's relatively high exposure to the energy sector has helped drive its outperformance this year. The Iran war initially drove up oil prices, fueling a rally in oil stocks. For example, shares of ExxonMobil and Chevron are up 14% and 11%, respectively, this year, outperforming the S&P 500.

However, oil isn't the only factor driving HDV's quiet outperformance this year. The fund has also benefited from its high exposure to healthcare stocks, notably AbbVie and Merck (NYSE: MRK), the latter of which is its 9th-largest holding at 4.1%. Shares of AbbVie have gained more than 14% while Merck has rallied over 23%. AbbVie reported a 12.4% increase in first-quarter revenue, driven by the strength of its immunology (up 16.4%) and neuroscience portfolios (up 26%). AbbVie also took a step to deepen its immunology portfolio by recently agreeing to acquire Apogee Therapeutics for $10.9 billion. Meanwhile, Merck reported 5% sales growth in the first quarter, driven by a 12% increase in Keytruda sales. The company also strengthened its oncology pipeline by acquiring Terns Pharmaceutical for $6.7 billion.

Story Continues

High-yielding dividend stocks can also yield higher returns

Many investors view high-yielding dividend stocks as lower-returning investments. However, that's not always the case. High-quality, high-yielding dividend stocks can often deliver high total returns as they grow their earnings and payouts. HDV's focus on high-quality dividend stocks such as Exxon, Chevron, AbbVie, and Merck has paid off this year as they've helped the fund quietly outperform the S&P 500. With more income and growth ahead, the iShares Core High Dividend ETF should continue to quietly deliver strong returns (it has delivered more than 10% annualized returns over the last one-, three-, and five-year periods as well as since its inception in 2011).

news.Title: AppLovin Corporation (APP) Stock Down Heavily Since Jim Cramer Was вҖңComfortableвҖқ Recommending It
news.Content:
We recently published Jim Cramer's Biggest Losers: 10 Stocks That Just Didn't Work Out . AppLovin Corporation (NASDAQ:APP) is one of the stocks discussed by Jim Cramer.

AppLovin Corporation (NASDAQ:APP) is a technology company that enables customers to run digital advertisements. Its shares are up by 52% over the past year and are down by 14.8% year-to-date. The stock closed 4.5% higher on June 29th. On that day, Raymond James initiated coverage of the firm to set a Strong Buy rating and a $640 share price target. The financial firm discussed AppLovin Corporation (NASDAQ:APP)'s move into eCommerce advertising and the integration of AI into its operations as some of the reasons behind its optimism. Other factors that have impacted the stock include Google's Project Genie and competition from social media giant Meta, with some reports suggesting that the stock has struggled due to these. AppLovin Corporation (NASDAQ:APP)'s shares closed 16.9% lower on January 30th, while on January 29th, Google's Genie launch enabled users to create and interact in digital worlds. Cramer discussed AppLovin Corporation (NASDAQ:APP) in detail on January 5th:

"The eighth-best stock in the Nasdaq-100 was AppLovin. Now, that's an advertising software company that helps its customers, like many mobile game developers, grow their reach and monetize their platforms. Now, this is another one with a big retail following. The stock put up huge gains earlier in the year before flattening out over the past few months, but did finish 2025 up 108%. Of courseвҖҰ some people would call these cult stocks. AppLovin's expensive, trading at 43 times this year's earnings estimates, which only seems cheap in comparison to Palantir. That said, this company has legitimately great growth and the business is incredibly profitable. Right now, you know what, I can't name a single competitor to AppLovin. It's like this company has the whole market to itself. Specifically, AppLovin has seen its revenue roughly triple in the past four years. Meanwhile, the earnings per share have gone from next to nothing to an expected $9.37 for 2025, which would be more than double 2024's number. The best part, AppLovin's growth is expected to continue or even accelerate. Wall Street's looking for 37% revenue growth and 56% earnings growth. And that's why I'm more comfortable recommending this one because even though AppLovin trades like a speculative stock, the business really isn't that speculative."

While we acknowledge the potential of APP as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. news.Title: Moderna Stock Is Soaring. Is It Too Late to Buy?
news.Content:
Moderna 's (NASDAQ: MRNA) share price has more than doubled this year as the market has grown increasingly optimistic about the company's pipeline and encouraging regulatory progress for its flu vaccine. But after such a sharp rally, it's fair to ask whether there's still an opportunity here to make some money.

What comes next for Moderna?

The investment case for Moderna isn't just about COVID-19 vaccines anymore. It's about what comes next.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue В»

Today, the company has three commercial products, multiple late-stage vaccine programs, and an expanding pipeline that stretches well beyond infectious diseases. Moderna is developing personalized cancer vaccines in partnership with Merck and pursuing therapies for rare genetic disorders.

The company is now preparing for multiple product launches in 2027 and 2028, including seasonal flu, a flu/COVID-19 combination vaccine, and a norovirus vaccine. That's a dramatically different company than the one investors knew during the pandemic, when nearly all of its revenue came from a single COVID-19 vaccine. If even a handful of these late-stage programs reach the market, Moderna could become a much more diversified biotechnology company with multiple sources of recurring revenue. And the financial picture is improving, too.

Possible breakeven by 2028

During the first quarter of 2026, Moderna generated $389 million in revenue, up from $108 million a year earlier. Although the company still posted a net loss, it finished the quarter with approximately $7.5 billion in cash and investments, providing it with ample resources to continue funding its research pipeline. Management also continues targeting up to 10% revenue growth in 2026 while working toward cash breakeven by 2028.

The biggest near-term catalyst may be the company's flu vaccine . An FDA advisory committee recently voted 9-0 to recommend approval for Moderna's seasonal influenza vaccine for adults 50 and older. While the FDA isn't required to follow the panel's recommendation, it often does, with a final decision expected in early August. Of course, that doesn't mean the stock is without risk.

High expectations for Moderna

Much of Moderna's recent rally reflects higher expectations, and several important clinical readouts (including late-stage melanoma data) are still ahead. Any disappointing results could quickly change investor sentiment. But it's still hard to justify passing on the stock, even after its latest run.

Story Continues

You see, Moderna is no longer just a play on COVID-19. It's actually becoming a highly successful, diversified biotechnology company with multiple opportunities to create value over the next several years. After such a strong run, don't expect the shares to move in a straight line. But if you're thinking long-term, Moderna still offers considerable upside.

news.Title: Zacks Industry Outlook Highlights Micron, Seagate, Hewlett Packard and Agilysys
news.Content:
For Immediate Release

Chicago, IL вҖ“ July 7, 2026 вҖ“ Today, Zacks Equity Research discusses Micron Technology MU, Seagate Technology STX, Hewlett Packard HPE and Agilysys AGYS.

Industry: Integrated Technology Solutions

Link: https://www.zacks.com/commentary/2948557/4-stocks-to-buy-from-a-prospering-technology-solutions-industry

Players operating within the Zacks Computer вҖ“ Integrated Systems industry, including Micron Technology , Seagate Technology , Hewlett Packard and Agilysys , are reaping the benefits of several favorable industry trends like advancements in data management capabilities, a rapid shift away from traditional siloed systems toward more integrated deployment techniques and heightened demand for modern application development approaches. However, the industry is still recovering from significant headwinds stemming from persistent supply chain bottlenecks, a challenging macroeconomic climate characterized by rising inflation and higher interest rates, soaring prices for key inputs and delays in customer acceptance of new products and services. These factors have resulted in significant order backlogs across the industry, casting a shadow on its prospects.

Industry Description

The Zacks Computer - Integrated Systems industry comprises companies that deliver advanced information technology solutions spanning computer systems, software platforms, data storage infrastructure and microelectronics. These industry players are ramping up investments in data modernization and analytics, cybersecurity and threat defense, remote work enablement, process automation, contactless service delivery models, enhanced customer and employee experience offerings and supply chain modernization initiatives, which are aimed at accelerating digital transformation services for enterprise customers.

Some players provide technological solutions (products and services) to help organizations connect, interact and transact with customers. Others develop and market information recognition, data entry software, systems and technologies.

4 Computer - Integrated Systems Industry Trends in Focus

Integrated Solutions Driving Demand : The industry is experiencing a surge in demand for integrated solutions across enterprises of all scales, driven by increasing investments in cutting-edge software technologies, such as the Internet of Things (IoT), big data analytics, artificial intelligence (AI) and blockchain. Significant opportunities presented by business analytics, cloud computing, mobile technologies, security solutions and social business platforms are tailwinds. Additionally, industry players are anticipated to benefit from the recovering global IT spending, enabling them to capitalize on the rising demand for comprehensive and seamless integrated solutions that can streamline operations and enhance productivity across various sectors.

Story Continues

Solid Adoption of Multi-Cloud Model : The industry is witnessing the robust adoption of the multi-cloud model as enterprises seek to achieve better scalability and optimize resource utilization. This trend is expanding the scope of industry participants, enabling them to leverage the benefits of cloud and hardware/software virtual technologies, which are anticipated to favor the industry's growth. Moreover, as growth and investment opportunities in developed countries continue to slow down, emerging economies are poised to play a crucial role in driving the industry's future. The multi-cloud model's increasing popularity, coupled with the tailwinds from cloud and virtual technologies and the potential of emerging markets, presents a strong foundation for industry participants to capitalize on new opportunities and foster sustained growth.

Supply-Chain Bottlenecks and Backlogs : Industry participants are grappling with a multitude of challenges, including supply constraints, softening demand for servers and cognitive applications, as well as delays in customer acceptance. These factors have contributed to consistent backlog levels, particularly in the Compute, High-Performance Computing & Mass Storage Class and Storage segments. Furthermore, the industry's outlook is affected by the volatility in foreign exchange rates, primarily due to the prevailing macroeconomic scenario and headwinds in emerging markets.

Semiconductor Chip Shortage Mars Prospects : The industry is grappling with the ripple effects of the ongoing semiconductor chip shortage, which has posed significant challenges for participants. The time-consuming business model transition to cloud computing has compounded these difficulties, requiring companies to navigate complex operational shifts amid supply chain disruptions. Moreover, the prospects of industry players are further dampened by lower spending across datacenter systems, primarily due to component shortages, particularly in memory and CPUs, as well as a deceleration in hyperscale spending.

Zacks Industry Rank Indicates Bright Prospects

The Zacks Computer вҖ“ Integrated Systems industry is housed within the broader Zacks Computer and Technology sector. It carries a Zacks Industry Rank #13, which places it in the top 5% of more than 250 Zacks industries.

The group's Zacks Industry Rank, which is the average of the Zacks Rank of all the member stocks, indicates outperformance in the near term. Our research shows that the top 50% of the Zacks-ranked industries outperform the bottom 50% by a factor of more than 2 to 1.

The industry's position in the top 50% of the Zacks-ranked industries is a result of a positive earnings outlook for the constituent companies in aggregate. Looking at the aggregate earnings estimate revisions, it appears that analysts are optimistic about this group's earnings growth potential. Since July 30, 2025, the industry's earnings estimate for 2026 has moved north by 241.5%.

Before we present a few stocks that you may want to consider for your portfolio, let's take a look at the industry's recent stock-market performance and valuation picture.

Industry Beats Sector & S&P 500

The Zacks Computer вҖ“ Integrated Systems industry has outperformed the broader Computer and Technology sector and the Zacks S&P 500 composite in the past year.

The industry has returned 125.8% over this period compared with the S&P 500 and the broader Computer and Technology sector's respective growth of 22.6% and 33.8%.

Industry's Current Valuation

On the basis of the forward 12-month P/S, which is a commonly used multiple for valuing computer-integrated systems stocks, we see that the industry is currently trading at 6.18X compared with the S&P 500's 5.03X. It is also below the sector's forward 12-month P/S of 6.88X.

Over the past five years, the industry has traded as high as 7.08X and as low as 1.94X, with the median being at 3.12X.

4 Computer-Integrated Systems Providers to Buy

Micron Technology is well-positioned for near-term momentum, supported by compelling guidance and a flurry of strategic announcements. For fourth-quarter fiscal 2026, the company has guided revenues of $50.0 billion, with gross margins expanding to approximately 86% and non-GAAP EPS of $31. Executing on 16 multi-year Strategic Customer Agreements вҖ” including pacts with Anthropic (June 2026) and General Motors (July 2026) вҖ” strengthens revenue durability and visibility.

On the product front, HBM4 on 1-beta DRAM technology is in high-volume shipments, while 256GB DDR5 RDIMMs built on 1-gamma technology shipped to key server ecosystem enablers in May 2026. The newly operational Manassas, VA, fab adds critical domestic manufacturing capacity, aligning supply with growing AI-driven demand across data center, automotive and mobile end-markets.

MU currently sports a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy). You can see the complete list of today's Zacks #1 Rank stocks here.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for its fiscal 2026 earnings has moved north by 26.3% to $73.86 per share in the past 60 days. MU shares have gained 187.4% in the past six-month period.

Seagate Technology is favorably positioned for meaningful near-term growth, underpinned by strong fundamentals and management's clear guidance. Fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 revenues are guided at $3.45 billion with non-GAAP EPS of $5, reflecting meaningful sequential improvement over the fiscal third quarter's $3.11 billion revenues and record 47% non-GAAP gross margin. AI-driven cloud storage demand, supported by Seagate's areal density-led product innovation, is expected to sustain growth into fiscal 2027.

In June 2026, Seagate announced early redemption of its 3.50% Exchangeable Senior Notes due 2028, a clear signal of balance sheet confidence. A quarterly dividend of 74 cents, payable July 7, 2026, further underscores capital return discipline. Management targets continued sequential revenue and margin expansion through fiscal 2027, establishing STX as a well-positioned near-term opportunity.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for this Zacks Rank #1 company's fiscal 2026 earnings has moved north by 0.3% to $14.93 per share in the past 60 days. STX shares have gained 166.1% in the past six-month period.

Hewlett Packard Enterprise presents a compelling near-term investment case. In May 2026, HPE completed the H3C divestiture, generating $1.357 billion in proceeds and sharpening strategic focus. Second-quarter fiscal 2026 results (June 1) showed record revenues of $10.7 billion, up 40% YoY, with non-GAAP EPS of 79 cents and free cash flow of $0.9 billion.

HPE raised fiscal 2026 guidance to revenue growth of 29-33%, non-GAAP EPS of $3.35-$3.45, and free cash flow of at least $3.5 billion, each surpassing its fiscal 2028 targets. At HPE Discover Las Vegas (June 15-18), the company unveiled an agentic AI architecture and self-driving networking. Private Cloud AI's multi-node inference for 256 GPUs launches July 2026. Fiscal 2027 guidance projects 8-12% revenue growth and free cash flow exceeding $4.5 billion.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for this Zacks Rank #1 company's fiscal 2026 earnings has moved north by 41.5% to $3.41 per share in the past 60 days. HPE shares have returned 83.9% in the past six-month period.

Agilysys presents a compelling near-term investment case underpinned by three converging catalysts. First, the company's May 2026 INSPIRE conference unveiled over 30 AI-powered features built around four strategic pillars вҖ” Multi-Modal User Experiences, Hyper-Personalization, Agentic Process Automation and Revenue Intelligence вҖ” with early customer deployments expected within 90 days, accelerating product monetization.

Second, management's fiscal 2027 guidance targets total revenues of $365-$370 million, subscription revenue growth of at least 30% and adjusted EBITDA margin expansion to 24% from 21.2% in fiscal 2026, reflecting meaningful operating leverage ahead. Third, the cloud-native SaaS model вҖ” supported by fiscal 2026 recurring revenues of $205.9 million and annual free cash flow of $68.1 million вҖ” ensures durable earnings visibility. Two AI-native modules, Revenue Intelligence and a next-generation CRS, add an incremental near-term upsell opportunity.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for this Zacks Rank #1 company's fiscal 2027 earnings has increased by 9.2% to $2.37 per share in the past 60 days. AGYS shares have lost 6.8% in the past six-month period.

news.Title: Michaels Crafts an Unlikely Comeback Under Apollo
news.Content:
(Bloomberg) -- When Michaels Cos. tapped a new CEO in early 2025, the company's chairman, 25-year Apollo Global Management Inc. veteran Andrew Jhawar heralded the moment as "an exciting period of opportunity" for the retailer.

Most Read from Bloomberg

Greece Offers Bounty to Catch Ravenous Fish Lured by Warming Sea

Microsoft's Xbox to Cut 3,200 Jobs, Divest Five Studios in Major Overhaul

Two Millennium Trading Pods Made About $3.7 Billion Last Month

Saudis Slash Main Oil Price to Rare Discount as Market Dives

China Sentences Official to Death Over $325 Million in Bribes

As the dust settles some 16 months later, that's looking like an understatement.

Michaels' overhaul is readily apparent in stores, where balloons that were once a hallmark of bankrupt Party City now line the entrances. The company also swooped in to buy the intellectual property and brands of fabrics retailer Joann, which collapsed into Chapter 11 twice in the span of a year, adding yarn and sewing machines to its inventory.

Meanwhile on Wall Street, Michaels accomplished a rare feat: The company's bonds traded as low as 34 cents on the dollar last year, a level that usually indicates a restructuring is inevitable, but roared back close to 100 cents, giving it the chance to bolster its finances through the credit markets.

Some observers chalk up the swift maneuvers to the fact that Michaels is owned by Apollo, the investment giant that made its name by throwing sharp elbows in distressed investing and corporate turnarounds. The firm bought the retailer five years ago, meaning it's coming up on the time when private equity starts looking for an exit. For Michaels, that would most likely be an initial public offering, people familiar with the matter said, though no timeline has been set for a possible listing.

In the meantime that new chief executive officer, David Boone, is focused on opening re-imagined stores, with features designed to appeal to customers' desire for hands-on experiences and customization. The first opened this month in Columbus, Ohio, and will bolster the company's offerings of in-person events like junk journal making and watercolor cards. More will open later this year.

Michaels "had a reputation for being your grandmother's store," Boone, 57, said, while the new features make for "a complete transformation of this brand to be much more culturally relevant." The chance to build with some of its rivals' offerings was, he said, "a once-in-a-generation opportunity for the company."

The rush to capture market share has also revitalized Michaels' bottom line. Its first quarter sales and adjusted Ebitda both grew by double digits, according to people familiar with the matter. Inventory swelled by 20% as the company gets ready for the busier second half, they said, asking not to be identified discussing private financial details.

Story Continues

For now, Michaels' leverage remains high, and was cited as a potential credit challenge for the company by ratings agency Moody's Corp. in a February report. The credit grader, which rates Michaels' overall credit outlook as stable, expects the company's leverage to improve to 5.5x this year. Free cash flow is expected to be positive for 2026.

Representatives for Apollo and Michaels declined to comment on specific financial details.

'More Decisive'

Being privately held means Michaels has been able to move faster and make riskier bets than its public rivals, said Neil Saunders, a retail analyst at GlobalData. That gave it an advantage as debt-laden public companies like Joann and Party City started to fail, even as each business faced the same challenges: inflationary pressure, a slump in discretionary spending and competition from e-commerce giants like Amazon.com Inc. and Walmart Inc.

"If you are owned by private equity, you can just generally be a much more decisive business," Saunders said. "You don't have to work through everything that a public business would have to work through."

In Apollo, Michaels had the backing of one of the world's largest alternative asset managers to help it expand product lines like yarn and fabric, which were a natural add-on to what customers would already be looking for in store. The closely held retailer was also insulated from the pressure of delivering quarterly results.

Just two months after Joann's second bankruptcy in January 2025, Michaels hosted a conference call with nearly a hundred vendors, including many who'd supplied the defunct store with fabrics and were looking for a new retailer. It urged them to work with Michaels to bring their products into its store as quickly as possible, instead of shopping around.

"If somebody exits the market or restructures, those sales tend to get dispersed among lots of different players in the market," said Boone, who previously led Staples Canada.

The move was just part of the retailer's strategy to go after customers before others could take advantage of the moment. Michaels also sped up its product timeline, bringing new items into stores without the usual pilot schemes and trial periods that could stretch into a multi-year process, Boone said in an interview.

But as the company was moving fast, so was President Donald Trump. In early April 2025, less than three months after his second inauguration, the president laid out plans for the largest US tariffs on imported goods in modern history. The US and China вҖ” where a huge share of Michaels' merchandise is made вҖ” volleyed new duties back and forth, with taxes on imports from China at one point hitting 125%.

The announcement sparked crisis calls inside the company over tariffs that could have led to a liquidity crunch, according to a person familiar with the matter. In May, Michaels' bonds due 2029 fell to trade at just 34 cents on the dollar. During this period, Apollo took on a much more hands-on role than usual, talking on the phone with Michaels' management several times a day for multiple hours, according to another person familiar with the matter.

Luckily for Michaels, the US and China agreed on a significant reduction of tariffs in mid May, the company's bonds rallied and restructuring talk was put aside. Now, Apollo's representatives speak to Michaels' management on a regular weekly call, with occasional ad hoc check-ins to share information about the industry or a competitor, the person said.

The turmoil came less than two months after Boone took the CEO role, and in the middle of an auction for Joann's intellectual property. Despite the backdrop, Michaels and Apollo were keen to outbid rivals, and ultimately paid less than $10 million to clinch a deal for the Joann name and some of its private labels, said the person familiar.

After the deal, it launched a dedicated landing page for Joann's customers looking for their favorite products, including a large welcome banner. "It's hard to lose a store you love," the banner read. "We're here to help."

Craft Circles

On a recent Thursday in a Michaels store in Brooklyn, New York, aisles close to the entrance were stacked with Memorial Day decor. Stark, fluorescent lighting lined the ceilings, but not all of them were on, leaving parts of the store brightly illuminated while other sections were dim.

In one of the aisles upstairs, Nicole Itzkowitz was browsing for fabric dye so she could refresh her canvas bag instead of buying a new one. Itzkowitz, 31, said she'd been in Michaels just a week earlier, too, buying knitting needles for one of the craft circles she's a part of.

"As I'm getting older, I'm not going out the same way I was, but not going out you lose some of the social connections," said Itzkowitz, a PhD student at Columbia University's Mailman School of Public Health, who joined the knitting groups as a new way to socialize.

Almost three-quarters of adults in the US participated in at least one craft activity last year, according to market research firm Mintel Ltd. Crafting grew in popularity during the pandemic, and has stayed on trend as increasing digital fatigue motivates people to seek more analog hobbies.

"People are interested in those sorts of quieter, more fulfilling types of activities in what is a very noisy world," retail analyst Saunders said. "I think that's Michaels' sweet spot."

Michaels continues to face a host of other competitors, both from dedicated arts-and-craft chains like Hobby Lobby Stores Inc. and retail giants like Amazon and Walmart. But Boone said what sets Michaels apart is the sheer variety of products available in its stores for customers to choose from, in person.

Tasha Washington-Lee, 55, who was shopping at the Brooklyn Michaels for yarn to build a doll, agrees. Washington-Lee said she likes how at Michaels you can get sharpies in every skin color.

Higher Costs

But as Michaels works to bring more items to its stores, the company also faces higher costs. Despite efforts to diversify its supply chains away from high-tariff countries вҖ” its custom frames, for example, are manufactured in Texas and North Carolina, and it's working on making more party supplies locally вҖ” the company still has significant exposure to China, according to an S&P Global report.

Any additional tariffs on imports from China could hurt margins, given the company's limited ability to pass on price increases to already stressed consumers, Moody's analysts wrote in their February report.

The war in Iran has also driven up fuel costs and made moving goods more expensive. To save on fuel, Boone said, Michaels is loading more onto each of its delivery trucks while reducing the number of trips the trucks make.

What matters most, he said, is figuring out what customers want most and delivering those products quickly вҖ” without getting distracted by items that shoppers don't care about.

"They come because they have a passion for it," Boone said. "So we are working very hard culturally to live up to that standard."

The Fun Shortage Is Real, and It's Making America Miserable

'Southern Squeeze' Grips US Cities Once Known for Affordability

At 17, She Sued Meta and Google, and Won. Now She's Ready to Tell Her Story

Which Companies Actually Use AI? A New Index Has Answers

news.Title: AI Leaders Nvidia, Palantir, and Meta Platforms Are Shaking Wall Street's Foundation With This $15.6 Billion Warning
news.Content:
No trend has captured the attention and capital of investors since the start of this century quite like the evolution of artificial intelligence (AI) . Giving software and systems the tools to make autonomous, split-second decisions is a technology that PwC analysts believe can add $15.7 trillion to the global economy by 2030.

Nvidia 's (NASDAQ: NVDA) graphics processing units (GPUs) are the catalyst for this technological revolution and are what make AI data centers tick. Meanwhile, software-as-a-service pioneer Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) and social media maven Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) are at the forefront of deploying AI applications to boost their bottom lines.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue В»

Palantir's AI platforms are critical to the United States' military operations, while Meta has integrated generative AI into its ad platforms to allow businesses to tailor their messages to users.

But things may not be as perfect for these AI leaders as their share prices suggest .

Nvidia's, Palantir's, and Meta's insiders are sending an unmistakable warning to Wall Street

Although dozens of Wall Street analysts follow every move this AI trio makes, no one knows these businesses better than their insiders. An insider is a high-ranking executive, board member, or beneficial owner of at least 10% of outstanding shares, who may possess non-public information.

For the sake of transparency, as well as to satisfy securities law, insiders are required to file Form 4 with the Securities and Exchange Commission no more than two business days after transacting in their company's stock. This includes exercising options.

According to aggregated Form 4 filings, the insiders at Wall Street's most influential AI businesses have been decisive net sellers of their company's stock over the trailing three-year period (through July 3, 2026):

Nvidia : $5,088,590,417 in net selling

Palantir : $6,037,205,924 in net selling

Meta Platforms : $4,489,819,191 in net selling

Altogether, insiders at these three titans have dumped a net of more than $15.6 billion of their company's stock.

The asterisk to the above Form 4 data is that not all insider selling is necessarily nefarious. Since most high-ranking executives and board members are paid in stock and/or options, they'll be required to sell a portion of what they receive to cover their federal and/or state tax liability. Pre-planned selling for tax purposes isn't something that should concern Wall Street or investors.

Story Continues

But the other side of this coin tells an equally compelling story.

While there's been plenty of selling, insider buying activity has been virtually nonexistent for this trio over the trailing three years:

Nvidia : $250,000 in insider buying

Palantir : $7,837,856 in insider buying

Meta : $0 insider buying

Not one penny has been spent by Meta's insiders to purchase shares, while Wall Street's largest publicly traded company has seen only $250,000 in insider buying.

The reason insiders aren't buying may have to do with historical precedent. Every game-changing technology for more than 30 years has endured an early innings bubble-bursting event. These bubbles form because investors persistently overestimate the adoption and optimization timeline of new technologies.

Although Palantir and Meta have set the bar high for AI applications, and no chip developers have come close to matching the compute capabilities of Nvidia's GPUs, we're not particularly close to companies optimizing their sales and profits from AI solutions.

History provides a warning that insiders at some of the most prominent AI companies appear to be heeding.

news.Title: The SpaceX Sell-Off Looks to Be Getting Worse. Here's What Patient Investors Should Do Right Now.
news.Content:
After a debut that sent the stock to a peak near $226 per share within days, Space Exploration Technologies (NASDAQ: SPCX) has given back a large chunk of that run and trades near $160 as of this writing. The pullback has rattled some new shareholders, and the honest read is that it might have more room to fall. That does not make the stock a mistake. It makes the method you use to buy it the thing that matters.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue В»

Why the SpaceX sell-off might get worse

The first pressure is supply. SpaceX staggered its insider lockup, and the first block of shares, nearly 20% of the locked pool, is released after the second-quarter report in late July. Smaller tranches will follow through the fall , with the full 180-day batch clearing in December. More sellable shares meeting the same pool of buyers can press the price lower, and that supply arrives on a schedule the market can see coming.

The second pressure is the price itself. A market value near $2 trillion bakes in moon bases, a high Starship flight rate, and orbital data centers, outcomes that could take a decade to prove. A launch setback or a slipped timeline could reset sentiment in a hurry, and the $226 per-share peak looked more like debut momentum than a considered price. Momentum fades, and a stock that tripled expectations in a week can keep giving back ground.

Why dollar-cost averaging is the move

Instead of one lump purchase at a price no one can predict, dollar-cost averaging commits a fixed dollar amount on a set schedule, month after month, at whatever the price allows. When shares drop, that fixed sum buys more of them; when shares climb, it buys fewer. Across a volatile stock with a known supply calendar, the math lowers your average cost and removes the pressure to call a bottom that no one can call. For a company with a decade-long story and lockups draining out through December, spreading purchases across those same months lines up with the supply.

The approach has limits worth stating. If the stock climbs in a straight line, a single lump-sum buy would have beaten it. And dollar-cost averaging does not fix a weak business; it addresses timing, nothing more. The case rests on the belief that SpaceX is worth owning for years, with the investing schedule managing volatility along the way.

Story Continues

The takeaway for patient investors

The sell-off is uncomfortable, and discomfort is where a patient plan earns its keep. Set an amount you can add on a schedule, tune out the daily move, and let the lockup-driven supply come to you rather than chasing the stock. A brokerage account that supports recurring buys makes the habit automatic. I would treat SpaceX as a position built across quarters, not a trade timed to a bottom.

news.Title: еҸ°иӮЎйҮҚжҢ«еҚғй»һи·Ңз ҙжңҲз·ҡ ж¬ҠеҖји»ҚеңҳеҖ’жҲҗдёҖзүҮпјҒеңӢжі°йҮ‘й ҳйҮ‘иһҚеӨ§е…өйҖҶйўЁж’җзҙ…иүІйҳІз·ҡпҪңYahooиІЎз¶“жҺғжҸҸ
news.Content:
зҫҺеңӢеёӮе ҙеҸ—еҚҡйҖҡгҖҒи¶…еҫ®зӯүAIжҷ¶зүҮиӮЎиө°еј·пјҢжҗӯй…ҚжҲҙзҲҫиЎқй«ҳпјҢеё¶еӢ•зҫҺиӮЎдёүйҖЈдј‘еҫҢз§‘жҠҖиӮЎеј·еҪҲпјҢзҫҺеңӢеӣӣеӨ§жҢҮж•ё6ж—Ҙе…Ёйқўж”¶жјІпјҢе…¶дёӯйҒ“з“Ҡе·ҘжҘӯжҢҮж•ёеүөжӯ·еҸІж”¶зӣӨж–°й«ҳгҖӮеҖӢиӮЎж–№йқўпјҢе·қжҷ®зҷјиЁҖеҠӣи®ҡжҲҙзҲҫпјҢжҝҖеӢөжҲҙзҲҫиӮЎеғ№еӨ§жјІ4.43%пјӣеҚҡйҖҡиҲҮиҳӢжһңз°ҪзҪІй•·жңҹеҗҲзҙ„пјҢиӮЎеғ№дёҠжјІ3.73%пјҢд№ҹеё¶еӢ•д»Је·ҘеӨҘдјҙеҸ°з©Қйӣ»ADRеҗҢжӯҘи·іжјІ4.06%пјӣиҖҢй«ҳзӣӣзңӢеҘҪAMDз¬¬дәҢеӯЈиЎЁзҸҫдёҰиӘҝеҚҮзӣ®жЁҷеғ№пјҢAMDиӮЎеғ№йЈҶжјІ6.61%пјӣеҫ®и»ҹеүҮеӣ иЈҒе“Ўзҙ„2.1%гҖҒзҙ„4800еҗҚе“Ўе·ҘпјҢиӮЎеғ№е°Ҹи·Ң0.96%гҖӮ

дәһиӮЎж–№йқўпјҢдё»иҰҒеёӮе ҙеӨҡж•ёиө°ејұпјҢж—ҘиӮЎдёӢи·Ң2.12%пјҢйҹ“иӮЎйҮҚжҢ«4.91%пјҢзӣӨдёӯдёҖеәҰи·ҢиҮізҶ”ж–·пјӣжёҜиӮЎиө°и·Ң0.56%пјҢдёҠиӯүжҢҮж•ёдёӢи·Ң1.26%гҖӮ

еҸ°иӮЎд»Ҡ(7)ж—ҘеҸ—дәһиӮЎйҮҚжҢ«иҲҮеёӮе ҙжҒҗж…ҢиіЈеЈ“жӢ–зҙҜпјҢзӣӨдёӯеҠҮзғҲйңҮзӣӘйҖҫ1500й»һпјҢзөӮе ҙдёӢи·Ң1077.28й»һгҖҒи·Ңе№…2.31%пјҢж”¶45,479.11й»һпјҢжҲҗдәӨйҮ‘йЎҚж”ҫеӨ§иҮі1е…Ҷ1715.84е„„е…ғгҖӮж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎж–№йқўпјҢ еҸ°з©Қйӣ»(2330) иӮЎеғ№дёӢи·Ң20е…ғпјҢйӣ»еӯҗиӮЎжҢҮж•ёеҗҢжӯҘиө°ејұпјҢ иҒҜзҷјз§‘(2454) гҖҒ ж—ҘжңҲе…үжҠ•жҺ§(3711) гҖҒ иҒҜйӣ»(2303) дёӢжҢ«пјҢ еңӢе·Ё*(2327) еүҮжҳҜйҮҚжҢ«и·ҢеҒңгҖҒиЁҳжҶ¶й«”ж—ҸзҫӨд№ҹйҒӯиіЈеЈ“еҮәиІЁпјҢеЈ“жҠ‘еӨ§зӣӨиЎЁзҸҫпјӣй•·жҰ®йӣҶеңҳеүҮеӣ ејөеңӢиҸҜгҖҒејөеңӢж”ҝж¶үе…§з·ҡдәӨжҳ“ж¶ҲжҒҜпјҢ й•·жҰ®(2603) ж—©зӣӨдёҖеәҰйҮҚжҢ«пјҢйӣҶеңҳиӮЎеҗҢжӯҘй–ӢдҪҺгҖӮзӣёијғд№ӢдёӢпјҢйҮ‘иһҚиӮЎйҖҶеӢўж’җзӣӨпјҢйҮ‘иһҚжҢҮж•ёдёҠжјІ35.57й»һпјҢ еҮұеҹәйҮ‘(2883) гҖҒ еңӢжі°йҮ‘(2882) гҖҒ е…ғеӨ§йҮ‘(2885) зӯүиЎЁзҸҫзӣёе°Қеј·еӢўпјҢз”ҹжҠҖиӮЎд№ҹжңүиіҮйҮ‘йҖІй§җпјҢжҲҗзӮәзӣӨйқўе°‘ж•ёжҠ—и·Ңдә®й»һгҖӮж•ҙй«”дҫҶзңӢпјҢеҸ°иӮЎд»Ҡж—Ҙе‘ҲзҸҫйӣ»еӯҗж¬ҠеҖјиҲҮй«ҳжіўеӢ•йЎҢжқҗиӮЎжүҝеЈ“гҖҒйҮ‘иһҚиҲҮйҳІзҰҰж—ҸзҫӨж’җзӣӨзҡ„зөҗж§ӢгҖӮ

рҹ“қзӣӨй»һзҶұй–ҖиӮЎпјҡзҫӨзӣҠиҮәзҒЈеҠ ж¬ҠжӯЈ2гҖҒзҫӨеүөгҖҒеҸӢйҒ”

зҶұй–ҖжҲҗдәӨиӮЎж–№йқўпјҢETFиҲҮйқўжқҝиӮЎжҲҗзӮәзӣӨйқўдәӨжҠ•з„Ұй»һпјҢеүҚдә”жӘ”зҡҶж”¶й»‘гҖӮ зҫӨзӣҠиҮәзҒЈеҠ ж¬ҠжӯЈ2(00685L) жҲҗдәӨйҮҸд»Ҙ121.1иҗ¬ејөеұ…еҶ пјҢдё»еӣ д»ҠзӮәеҲҶжӢҶеҫҢжҒўеҫ©дәӨжҳ“йҰ–ж—ҘпјҢеҠ дёҠгҖҢжңҖдҫҝе®ңеҸ°иӮЎжӯЈдәҢETFгҖҚи©ұйЎҢзҷјй…өпјҢеҗёеј•еӨ§йҮҸжҠ•иіҮдәәгҖӮ

йқўжқҝиӮЎеүҮз”ұ зҫӨеүө(3481) гҖҒ еҸӢйҒ”(2409) ж“ йҖІжҲҗдәӨжҰңеүҚж®өпјҢе…©жӘ”иіЈеЈ“ж№§зҸҫпјҢеҗҢжӯҘиө°ејұгҖӮе…¶йӨҳзҶұй–ҖжҲҗдәӨиӮЎеүҮз”ұETFеҢ…иҫҰпјҢ дё»еӢ•зөұдёҖеҚҮзҙҡ50(00403A) гҖҒ е…ғеӨ§еҸ°зҒЈ50жӯЈ2(00631L) еҗҢжЁЈеҗҚеҲ—еүҚдә”пјҢйЎҜзӨәеӨ§зӣӨй«ҳжӘ”йңҮзӣӘд№ӢйҡӣпјҢETFд»ҚжҳҜеёӮе ҙиіҮйҮ‘йҖІеҮәиҲҮжҸӣжүӢй »з№Ғзҡ„йҮҚиҰҒжЁҷзҡ„гҖӮ

рҹ“ҢдёүеӨ§жі•дәәиІ·иіЈи¶…

дёүеӨ§жі•дәәд»Ҡж—ҘеҗҲиЁҲиіЈи¶…946.91е„„е…ғпјҢеӨ–иіҮе–®ж—ҘиіЈи¶…547.31е„„е…ғпјҢд»ҚжҳҜзӣӨйқўдё»иҰҒиіЈеЈ“дҫҶжәҗпјӣиҮӘзҮҹе•Ҷж–№йқўпјҢиіЈи¶…487.34е„„е…ғгҖӮзӣёијғдёӢпјҢжҠ•дҝЎйҖҶеӢўеҠ зўј87.73е„„е…ғпјҢжҢҒзәҢжү®жј”е…§иіҮжүҝжҺҘи§’иүІгҖӮж•ҙй«”дҫҶзңӢпјҢжҠ•дҝЎйӣ–йҖҶеӢўз«ҷиІ·ж–№пјҢдҪҶеӨ–иіҮиіЈеЈ“иҲҮиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶйҒҝйҡӘиӘҝзҜҖпјҢд»ҚжҳҜеЈ“жҠ‘еҸ°иӮЎеҸҚеҪҲеҠӣйҒ“зҡ„й—ңйҚөгҖӮ

вңЁжң¬ж—Ҙиӯ°йЎҢиӮЎпјҡйҮ‘иһҚиӮЎ

жң¬ж—Ҙиӯ°йЎҢиӮЎиҒҡз„ҰйҮ‘иһҚиӮЎйҖҶеӢўж’җзӣӨпјҢеҸ°иӮЎзӣӨйқўйӣ–жңүеӨҡжӘ”еҖӢиӮЎдёӢи·ҢпјҢдҪҶиіҮйҮ‘жҳҺйЎҜиҪүеҗ‘йҮ‘иһҚж—ҸзҫӨйҒҝйҡӘпјҢ еҮұеҹәйҮ‘(2883) зӣӨдёӯдёҖеәҰеј·жјІйҖҫ6%й ҳи»ҚпјҢ еңӢжі°йҮ‘(2882) гҖҒ е…ғеӨ§йҮ‘(2885) еҗҢжӯҘиө°еј·пјҢ еҜҢйӮҰйҮ‘(2881) гҖҒ зҺүеұұйҮ‘(2884) гҖҒ ж°ёиұҗйҮ‘(2890) гҖҒ еҸ°ж–°ж–°е…үйҮ‘(2887) зӯүд№ҹзӣёе°ҚжҠ—и·ҢпјҢжүӣиө·еӨҡй ӯеӨ§ж——пјӣеҲҶжһҗеё«жҢҮеҮә...... й»һжҲ‘зңӢжӣҙеӨҡ

рҹ‘ҘйўұйўЁеӨ©иӘ°еңЁдёҠзҸӯпјҹпјҡ

жң¬ж—Ҙи©ұйЎҢиҒҡз„ҰйўұйўЁеӨ©еҮәеӢӨпјҢyes123жұӮиҒ·з¶ІиӘҝжҹҘйЎҜзӨәпјҢй«ҳйҒ”81%еӢһе·ҘжӣҫжңүйўұйўЁеӨ©дёҠзҸӯз¶“й©—пјҢе…¶дёӯ65%жҳҜиў«иҖҒй—ҶжҲ–дё»з®ЎиҰҒжұӮеҲ°зҸӯпјҢеӘ’й«”е…¬й—ңгҖҒжү№зҷјйӣ¶е”®гҖҒйҶ«иӯ·з”ҹжҠҖжӣҙжҲҗзӮәгҖҢдёүеӨ§иӢҰе‘ҪиЎҢжҘӯгҖҚгҖӮи–ӘиіҮйғЁеҲҶпјҢиӘҝжҹҘд№ҹжҢҮеҮә...... й»һжҲ‘зңӢжӣҙеӨҡ

рҹ”ӯйҮҚеӨ§иІЎз¶“дәӢд»¶еұ•жңӣ

е‘ЁдёүеңӢйҡӣиІЎз¶“з„Ұй»һпјҢе…ҲзңӢзҫҺеңӢеёӮе ҙпјҢиҒҜжә–жңғе°Үе…¬еёғ6жңҲжңғиӯ°зҙҖйҢ„пјҢдёҰе…¬еёғ5жңҲж¶ҲиІ»иҖ…дҝЎиІёпјҢеҗҢжҷӮеёӮе ҙд№ҹе°Үй—ңжіЁEIAзҹӯжңҹиғҪжәҗеұ•жңӣе ұе‘ҠиҲҮ3е№ҙжңҹе…¬еӮөжӢҚиіЈзөҗжһңпјӣжӯҗжҙІж–№йқўпјҢжҡ«з„ЎйҮҚеӨ§ж•ёж“ҡе…¬еёғпјӣдәһеӨӘеҚҖж–№йқўпјҢж—Ҙжң¬е°Үе…¬еёғ5жңҲз¶“еёёеёіж”¶ж”ҜпјҢйҹ“еңӢд№ҹе°Үе…¬еёғ5жңҲз¶“еёёеёіж”¶ж”ҜпјҢзҙҗиҘҝиҳӯеӨ®иЎҢеүҮе°Үе…¬еёғеҲ©зҺҮжұәзӯ–пјӣйҷёжёҜжҫіж–№йқўпјҢLEAP East 2026е…Ёзҗғз§‘жҠҖеұ•жңғе°Үж–јйҰҷжёҜзҷ»е ҙгҖӮ

еҸ°зҒЈж–№йқўпјҢеңӢе…§з„Ұй»һеҢ…жӢ¬дёӯз ”йҷўз¬¬36ж¬ЎйҷўеЈ«жңғиӯ°гҖҒеҸ°зҒЈеүөжҠ•жҡЁз§ҒеӢҹжҠ•иіҮе№ҙжңғгҖҒдәһиіҮи«–еЈҮй«ҳйӣ„е ҙгҖҒе…¬е№іжңғе§”е“Ўжңғиӯ°пјҢд»ҘеҸҠз«Ӣжі•йҷўиІЎе§”жңғеҜ©жҹҘ115е№ҙеәҰдёӯеӨ®ж”ҝеәңзёҪй җз®—жЎҲйҷ„еұ¬е–®дҪҚй җз®—пјӣдјҒжҘӯеӢ•ж…Ӣж–№йқўпјҢжі•иӘӘжңғеҸҜз•ҷж„Ҹ еҸ°йҒ”йӣ»(2308) гҖҒ йҒ еӮі(4904) гҖҒ зҹҪеүө(8016) гҖҒ жҹҸйЁ°(3518) гҖҒ еҘ§зҫ©иіҪеҚҡ-KYеүө(7823) зӯүпјҢйҷӨж¬ҠжҒҜз„Ұй»һеүҮеҢ…жӢ¬ иҒҜиҸҜ(1229) гҖҒ еҚ—дәһ(1303) гҖҒ иҒҜйӣ»(2303) гҖҒ й•·жҰ®иҲӘ(2618) гҖҒ жјўзҝ”(2634) гҖҒ з·Ҝеүө(3231) гҖҒ йҲҠиұЎ(3293) гҖҒ йҒ еӮі(4904) зӯүпјҢй җжңҹе°ҮзүҪеӢ•зӣӨйқўиіҮйҮ‘ијӘеӢ•иҲҮеЎ«жҒҜиЎҢжғ…гҖӮ

еёӮе ҙи®ҠеҢ–йӣЈжҚүж‘ёпјҹйқўе°ҚиӮЎеёӮзҡ„еӨ§иө·еӨ§иҗҪпјҢзёҪжңғжңүзңӢдёҚжҮӮгҖҒи·ҹдёҚдёҠгҖҒйӣЈжұәзӯ–зҡ„жҷӮеҲ»пјҒ YahooиІЎз¶“жҺғжҸҸ жҜҸеҖӢеҸ°иӮЎдәӨжҳ“ж—Ҙжҷҡй–“6й»һдёҠиҸңпјҢж•ҙзҗҶеёӮе ҙжңҖйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„иІЎз¶“зҶұй»һпјҢеҫһеңӢйҡӣиІЎз¶“и¶ЁеӢўгҖҒз”ўжҘӯз„Ұй»һеҲ°еҖӢиӮЎз•°еӢ•пјҢиҝ…йҖҹжҺҢжҸЎжҠ•иіҮи„ҲеӢ•пјҢеё¶дҪ еҝ«дәәдёҖжӯҘпјҢжҺҢжҸЎеёӮе ҙе…Ҳж©ҹгҖӮ

иЁ»пјҡеҠ ж¬ҠжҢҮж•ёжҲҗдәӨйҮ‘йЎҚеҗ«зӣӨеҫҢе®ҡеғ№пјҢдёҚеҗ«йӣ¶иӮЎгҖҒйү…йЎҚгҖҒжӢҚиіЈеҸҠжЁҷиіј

пјҲж ёзЁҝз·ЁијҜпјҡеҠүеҮұйҠҳпјү

news.Title: Market Crash?: 3 Financial Stocks I'd Buy Hand Over Fist
news.Content: Is a market crash coming? That's a trick question. Because a market crash is almost certainly coming at some point, although no one can predict exactly when. The reason it's pertinent right now is that the market looks very rich, which can signal a crash coming sooner rather than later. The Shiller or CAPE ratio, which is the average S&P 500 price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio adjusted for inflation, at almost 42 has only been higher than it is today ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў The article discusses the possibility of a market crash and identifies three financial stocks (JPMorgan Chase, Visa, and Berkshire Hathaway) as potential buys during such an event.
вҖў JPMorgan Chase reported a 10% year-over-year revenue increase and a 13% net income rise in Q1 2026, with a strong balance sheet and a 1.8% dividend yield.
вҖў Visa saw a 17% year-over-year revenue increase and a 20% adjusted EPS rise in Q2 fiscal 2026, benefiting from its position in the global payments system.
вҖў Berkshire Hathaway is highlighted for its diverse portfolio of nearly 200 companies and a substantial equity portfolio worth $343 billion.
news.Keywords: market crash, financial stocks, JPMorgan Chase, Visa, Berkshire Hathaway

news.Title: еӨ§е’ҢзҳӢзӢӮдёҠдҝ®122%пјҒ еҚ—дәһз§‘зӣ®жЁҷеғ№йЈҶиҮі650е…ғ
news.Content:
иЁҳжҶ¶й«”з”ўжҘӯжҷҜж°ЈжҢҒзәҢеҚҮжә«пјҢDRAMеғ№ж јеј·еӢўиө°жҸҡпјҢеё¶еӢ•еёӮе ҙйҮҚж–°и©•еғ№зӣёй—ңеҖӢиӮЎгҖӮеҚ—дәһз§‘пјҲ2408пјүе…¬еёғ6жңҲзҮҹж”¶еҫҢиЎЁзҸҫдә®зңјпјҢе°Үж–ј7жңҲ10ж—ҘиҲүиЎҢз¬¬дәҢеӯЈжі•иӘӘжңғпјҢжңҖж–°жӣҙзҚІеӨ–иіҮеӨ§еҠӣжҢүи®ҡгҖӮеӨ§е’ҢиіҮжң¬иӯүеҲёжңҖж–°е ұе‘ҠжҢҮеҮәпјҢеңЁDRAMжјІеғ№ж•ҲжҮүжҢҒзәҢзҷјй…өдёӢпјҢеҚ—дәһз§‘зҮҹйҒӢеӢ•иғҪжҳҺйЎҜеўһеј·пјҢеӣ жӯӨе°Үзӣ®жЁҷеғ№з”ұеҺҹе…Ҳ292е…ғеӨ§е№…иӘҝеҚҮиҮі650е…ғпјҢиӘҝе№…й«ҳйҒ”122.6%пјҢдёҰйҮҚз”ігҖҢиІ·йҖІпјҲBuyпјүгҖҚи©•зӯүпјҢжҲҗзӮәеёӮе ҙй—ңжіЁз„Ұй»һгҖӮ

еӨ§е’ҢиіҮжң¬иЎЁзӨәпјҢеҚ—дәһз§‘з¬¬дәҢеӯЈзҮҹж”¶еҸ—жғ DRAMеғ№ж је…ЁйқўдёҠжјІпјҢзҮҹйҒӢжҲҗй•·еӢ•иғҪзӣёз•¶е®Ңж•ҙгҖӮйҡЁи‘—DDR4еғ№ж јжҢҒзәҢиө°й«ҳпјҢй җжңҹд»Ҡе№ҙдёӢеҚҠе№ҙзҚІеҲ©д»ҚжңүйҖІдёҖжӯҘдёҠдҝ®з©әй–“пјҢеғ№ж јжјІеӢўз”ҡиҮіжңүжңӣдёҖи·Ҝе»¶зәҢиҮі2027е№ҙгҖӮ

ж №ж“ҡйӣҶйӮҰпјҲTrendForceпјүжңҖж–°е ұе‘ҠпјҢжҲӘиҮі6жңҲеә•пјҢж¶ҲиІ»еһӢDRAMеёӮе ҙдҫӣзөҰдҫқиҲҠеҗғз·ҠпјҢйҷӨDDR4еғ№ж јжҢҒзәҢж”ҖеҚҮеӨ–пјҢDDR3гҖҒDDR2еғ№ж јеҗҢжӯҘжҳҺйЎҜдёҠжјІгҖӮеӨ§е’ҢеҲҶжһҗжҢҮеҮәпјҢдё»иҰҒеҺҹеӣ еңЁж–јдёүжҳҹгҖҒзҫҺе…үгҖҒSKжө·еҠӣеЈ«зӯүеңӢйҡӣиЁҳжҶ¶й«”еӨ§е» пјҢжҢҒзәҢе°Үз”ўиғҪиҪүеҗ‘й«ҳй »еҜ¬иЁҳжҶ¶й«”пјҲHBMпјүеҸҠдјәжңҚеҷЁDRAMпјҢе°ҺиҮҙеӮізөұDRAMдҫӣзөҰжҢҒзәҢж”¶зё®пјҢеғ№ж јдёҠжјІи¶ЁеӢўжӣҙеҠ жҳҺйЎҜпјҢй җдј°з¬¬дёүеӯЈPCз”ЁDRAMеғ№ж јеҸҜжңӣеҶҚеӯЈеўһзҙ„20%гҖӮ

еҸҰдёҖж–№йқўпјҢAIжөӘжҪ®д№ҹжҢҒзәҢжҺЁеҚҮдјҒжҘӯзҙҡе„ІеӯҳйңҖжұӮгҖӮйҡЁи‘—жҺЁи«–еһӢAIеҝ«йҖҹзҷјеұ•пјҢдјҒжҘӯзҙҡSSDйңҖжұӮеҗҢжӯҘеҚҮжә«пјҢеҠ дёҠй•·жңҹдҫӣиІЁеҚ”иӯ°пјҲLTAпјүж”Ҝж’җпјҢеёӮе ҙзңӢеҘҪз·©иЎқиЁҳжҶ¶й«”пјҲBuffer DRAMпјүйңҖжұӮе°Үз¶ӯжҢҒй«ҳжӘ”пјҢйңҖжұӮеӢ•иғҪеҸҜжңӣдёҖи·Ҝе»¶зәҢиҮі2027е№ҙз”ҡиҮіжӣҙй•·жҷӮй–“гҖӮ

еңЁзҚІеҲ©еұ•жңӣж–№йқўпјҢеӨ§е’ҢеҗҢжӯҘиӘҝй«ҳеҚ—дәһз§‘2026е№ҙиҮі2028е№ҙжҜҸиӮЎзЁ…еҫҢзҙ”зӣҠпјҲEPSпјүй җдј°пјҢиӘҝеҚҮе№…еәҰд»Ӣж–ј37%иҮі91%гҖӮе ұе‘Ҡй җдј°пјҢ2026е№ҙEPSеҸҜйҒ”41.10е…ғпјҢ2027е№ҙйҖІдёҖжӯҘж”ҖеҚҮиҮі58.55е…ғпјҢ2028е№ҙеүҮжңүжңӣйҒ”47.1е…ғпјҢеҸҚжҳ DRAMеғ№ж јдёҠжјІе°ҚзҚІеҲ©её¶дҫҶзҡ„еј·еӢҒжҺЁеҚҮж•ҲжһңгҖӮ

еҖјеҫ—жіЁж„Ҹзҡ„жҳҜпјҢйӣ–з„¶еҚ—дәһз§‘з®ЎзҗҶеұӨе…ҲеүҚжӣҫеӣ ж“ҙеӨ§иіҮжң¬ж”ҜеҮәпјҢйҮӢеҮәжңӘдҫҶиӮЎеҲ©ж”ҝзӯ–еҸҜиғҪи¶Ёж–јдҝқе®Ҳзҡ„иЁҠжҒҜпјҢдҪҶеӨ§е’ҢиӘҚзӮәпјҢеңЁзҚІеҲ©еҝ«йҖҹжҲҗй•·ж”Ҝж’җдёӢпјҢе…¬еҸё2027е№ҙиҮі2028е№ҙд»Қжңүж©ҹжңғз¶ӯжҢҒз”ҡиҮіжҸҗй«ҳ50%зҡ„й…ҚжҒҜзҺҮпјҢе…је…·жҲҗй•·иҲҮй…ҚжҒҜйЎҢжқҗгҖӮ

пјҲе°Ғйқўең–пјҸзҝ»ж”қиҮӘGoogle Mapsпјү

гҖҗ жң¬ж–Ү з”ұ жқұжЈ®иІЎз¶“ жҸҗдҫӣпјҢжңӘз¶“жҺҲж¬ҠпјҢи«ӢеӢҝиҪүијүпјҒгҖ‘

news.Title: еӨ–иіҮжёӣзўјеҸ°иӮЎ547е„„е…ғгҖҖеӨ§иіЈдё»еӢ•еһӢETF
news.Content:
пјҲдёӯеӨ®зӨҫиЁҳиҖ…йҚҫжҰ®еі°еҸ°еҢ—2026е№ҙ07жңҲ7ж—Ҙйӣ»пјүеҸ°иӮЎд»ҠеӨ©йҮҚжҢ«1077й»һзӮәеҸІдёҠз¬¬8еӨ§и·Ңй»һпјҢеӨұе®ҲжңҲз·ҡ45710й»һпјҢиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶеӨ§иіЈж–°еҸ°е№Ј487.33е„„е…ғпјҢеӨ–иіҮд№ҹжёӣзўј547.31е„„е…ғпјӣеӨ–иіҮдё»иҰҒиіЈи¶…йқўжқҝйӣҷиҷҺеҸҠдё»еӢ•еһӢETFпјҢзөұиЁҲеӨ–иіҮе’ҢиҮӘзҮҹе•Ҷе·ІйҖЈ4иіЈпјҢжҠ•дҝЎд»ҠеӨ©иІ·и¶…87.73е„„е…ғпјҢйҖЈ11иІ·гҖӮ

еңЁеҸ°жҢҮжңҹж·ЁйғЁдҪҚпјҢдёүеӨ§жі•дәәд»ҠеӨ©ж·Ёз©әе–®жёӣе°‘2101еҸЈиҮі8062еҸЈпјҢе…¶дёӯеӨ–иіҮж·Ёз©әе–®жёӣе°‘45еҸЈпјҢд»Қи¶…йҒҺ8иҗ¬еҸЈиҮі8иҗ¬42еҸЈгҖӮ

и§ҖеҜҹеӨ–иіҮиІ·иіЈи¶…еҖӢиӮЎжҺ’иЎҢпјҢж №ж“ҡеҸ°зҒЈиӯүеҲёдәӨжҳ“жүҖзөұиЁҲпјҢеӨ–иіҮд»ҠеӨ©иІ·и¶…йҖҫиҗ¬ејөзҡ„иӮЎзҘЁжңүеҫ©иҸҜеҜҢжҷӮдёҚеӢ•з”ўпјҲ00712пјүгҖҒеҸ°ж–°ж–°е…үйҮ‘гҖҒеҮұеҹәйҮ‘гҖҒдё»еӢ•зҫӨзӣҠзҫҺеңӢеўһй•·пјҲ00997AпјүгҖҒеңӢжі°йҮ‘гҖҒй•·жҰ®иҲӘгҖҒеҸ°дјҒйҠҖгҖҒзҺүеұұйҮ‘гҖҒз¬¬дёҖйҮ‘9жӘ”гҖӮ

еӨ–иіҮд»ҠеӨ©иіЈи¶…йҒҺ10иҗ¬ејөзҡ„иӮЎзҘЁеҢ…жӢ¬дё»еӢ•зөұдёҖеҚҮзҙҡ50пјҲ00403AпјүгҖҒдё»еӢ•зөұдёҖеҸ°иӮЎеўһй•·пјҲ00981AпјүгҖҒзҫӨеүө3жӘ”пјҢеҗ„йҒ”14.85иҗ¬ејөгҖҒзҙ„12иҗ¬ејөгҖҒ11.39иҗ¬ејөгҖӮ

еӨ–иіҮиіЈи¶…еүҚ10еҗҚиӮЎзҘЁйӮ„еҢ…жӢ¬еҸӢйҒ”гҖҒдё»еӢ•еҫ©иҸҜжңӘдҫҶ50пјҲ00991AпјүгҖҒдё»еӢ•зөұдёҖе…Ёзҗғеүөж–°пјҲ00988AпјүгҖҒдё»еӢ•еҜҢйӮҰеҸ°зҒЈйҫҚиҖҖпјҲ00405AпјүгҖҒе…ғеӨ§еҸ°зҒЈ50 (0050) гҖҒдё»еӢ•еңӢжі°еӢ•иғҪй«ҳжҒҜпјҲ00400AпјүгҖҒдё»еӢ•зҫӨзӣҠз§‘жҠҖеүөж–°пјҲ00992AпјүгҖӮ

еҸ°ж–°жҠ•йЎ§зёҪз¶“зҗҶй»ғж–Үжё…жҺҘеҸ—иЁҳиҖ…йӣ»иЁӘжҢҮеҮәпјҢеҸ°иӮЎзӣӨдёӯж”»е …еҠӣйҒ“иҪүејұгҖҒиЎҢжғ…иҷ•ж–јиў«еӢ•пјҢзҫҺе…ғиҝ‘жңҹзӣёе°Қеј·еӢўпјҢеӨ–иіҮдёҰжңӘеӣһжөҒпјҢ7жңҲжңҹй–“еӨ–иіҮжҢҒзәҢжҸӣиӮЎпјҢе…§иіҮзӮәеҸ°иӮЎиҝ‘жңҹдё»жөҒпјҢдёҚйҒҺе…§иіҮд№ҹжҢҒзәҢжҸӣиӮЎпјҢйЎһиӮЎијӘеӢ•пјҢеҖӢиӮЎиЎЁзҸҫе·®з•°жҳҺйЎҜгҖӮ

жңҹиІЁжі•дәәеҲҶжһҗпјҢеҸ°иӮЎд»ҠеӨ©дёҠдёӢйңҮзӣӘ1535й»һпјҢжӯӨжіўжҖҘж®әе°ҚиһҚиіҮз”ўз”ҹеЈ“еҠӣпјҢеёӮе ҙжғ…з·’жіўеӢ•пјҢзҹӯз·ҡзӣӨеӢўжҢҒзәҢйңҮзӣӘпјҢзұҢзўјйҮҚж–°жҙ—зүҢпјҢеӨ§зӣӨдёҚжҺ’йҷӨжҢҒзәҢжӢүеӣһдҝ®жӯЈпјҢйқңеҫ…ж¬ҠеҖјдё»жөҒй«ҳеғ№иӮЎжӯўи·Ңд»ҘеҸҠзұҢзўјжІүжҫұгҖӮ

news.Title: еҸ°иӮЎйҮҚжҢ«еҚғй»һ дёүеӨ§жі•дәәиіЈи¶…946е„„ з¶ІиҰӢ1ж•ёеӯ—еҳҶпјҡйӣЈжҖӘж«ғиІ·и·ҢзҲӣ
news.Content:
еҸ°иӮЎд»ҠпјҲ7пјүж—ҘеҶҚеәҰдёҠжј”й«ҳжӘ”жҖҘж®әжҲІзўјпјҢ11й»һйҒҺеҫҢиіЈеЈ“еҮәзұ пјҢжҢҮж•ёз”ұзҙ…зҝ»й»‘пјҢзөӮе ҙйҮҚжҢ«1077й»һпјҢж”¶еңЁ45479й»һпјҢи·Ңе№…2.31%пјҢзӣӨдёӯйңҮзӣӘи¶…йҒҺ1500й»һпјҢжҲҗдәӨеҖјж”ҫеӨ§иҮі1.16е…Ҷе…ғгҖӮдёүеӨ§жі•дәәеҗҢжӯҘз«ҷеңЁиіЈж–№пјҢеҗҲиЁҲиіЈи¶…946е„„е…ғпјҢе…¶дёӯеӨ–иіҮиіЈи¶…547е„„е…ғпјҢиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶжӣҙеӨ§з Қ487е„„е…ғгҖӮ

ж №ж“ҡиӯүдәӨжүҖе…¬еёғзҡ„дёүеӨ§жі•дәәиІ·иіЈи¶…иіҮж–ҷпјҢеӨ–иіҮеҸҠйҷёиіҮд»Ҡж—ҘиіЈи¶…547.31е„„е…ғпјҢе·ІйҖЈж—Ҙз«ҷеңЁиіЈж–№пјӣжҠ•дҝЎеүҮйҖҶеӢўиІ·и¶…87.73е„„е…ғпјҢжҢҒзәҢжү®жј”иӯ·зӣӨи§’иүІгҖӮдёҚйҒҺпјҢиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶиіЈеЈ“зӣёз•¶жІүйҮҚпјҢиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶиҮӘиЎҢиІ·иіЈиіЈи¶…126.09е„„е…ғпјҢеҠ дёҠйҒҝйҡӘж“ҚдҪңиіЈи¶…361.23е„„е…ғпјҢеҗҲиЁҲиіЈи¶…487.33е„„е…ғпјҢе№ҫд№ҺйҖјиҝ‘еӨ–иіҮиіЈи¶…иҰҸжЁЎгҖӮ

PTTиӮЎжқҝд№ҹиҝ…йҖҹжҺҖиө·зҶұиӯ°пјҢдёҚе°‘з¶ІеҸӢе°ҮеҺҹжң¬жңҹеҫ…зҡ„жі•иӘӘжңғиЎҢжғ…жҲІзЁұзӮәгҖҢзҙ…еҢ…иЎҢжғ…и®ҠзҷҪеҢ…иЎҢжғ…гҖҚгҖҒгҖҢжі•иӘӘжңғи®ҠжҲҗжі•жңғпјҢйҳҝеҪҢйҷҖдҪӣгҖҚпјҢеҸҚжҳ еёӮе ҙжғ…з·’жҖҘиҪүзӣҙдёӢгҖӮе…¶дёӯпјҢиҮӘзҮҹе•Ҷзҡ„еӨ§е№…иӘҝзҜҖжӣҙжҲҗзӮәиЁҺи«–з„Ұй»һпјҢз¶ІеҸӢзҙӣзҙӣз•ҷиЁҖиЎЁзӨәпјҢгҖҢиҮӘзҮҹзңҹзӢ гҖҚгҖҒгҖҢиғҢйӘЁд»”гҖҚгҖҒгҖҢиҮӘзҮҹиіЈжі•е°ұжҳҜйҖҷжЁЈгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢд»ҠеӨ©дёӯе°ҸиӮЎи·ҢжҲҗйҖҷжЁЈе°ұжҳҜиҮӘзҮҹж®әзҡ„гҖҚгҖҒгҖҢиҮӘзҮҹиіЈзҡ„жҜ”еӨ–иіҮйӮ„е…ҮгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢйӣЈжҖӘOTCи·ҢзҲӣгҖҚпјҢиӘҚзӮәзӣӨдёӯжҖҘи·ҢиҲҮиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶеӨ§иҲүиӘҝзҜҖи„«дёҚдәҶй—ңдҝӮгҖӮ

д№ҹжңүжҠ•иіҮдәәжҢҒзәҢй—ңжіЁеӨ–иіҮеӢ•еҗ‘пјҢз•ҷиЁҖжҢҮеҮәгҖҢеӨ–иіҮйҖЈиіЈе№ҫеӨ©дәҶпјҹгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢеҸ°е№Јз№јзәҢиІ¶пјҢеӨ–иіҮз№јзәҢйӣўе®¶еҮәиө°гҖҚгҖҒгҖҢе°Ҹе…’дёҚиҰҒеҶҚеҖ’дәҶгҖҚпјҢж“”жҶӮеӨ–иіҮжҢҒзәҢжҸҗж¬ҫжҒҗи®“еёӮе ҙжүҝеЈ“гҖӮ

еҸҰдёҖж–№йқўпјҢжҠ•дҝЎйҖҶеӢўиІ·и¶…еүҮиў«йғЁеҲҶз¶ІеҸӢиҰ–зӮәжҢҒзәҢжүҝжҺҘеёӮе ҙиіЈеЈ“пјҢжңүдәәиЎЁзӨәгҖҢеӨ§е“ҘдёҖжӯҘйғҪжІ’жңүйҖҖгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢжҠ•дҝЎжҜҸеӨ©дҪҺиІ·й«ҳиіЈжҠ„еә•иіәзҲӣгҖҚпјҢдҪҶд№ҹжңүдәәж“”еҝғиӢҘеӨ–иіҮгҖҒиҮӘзҮҹжҢҒзәҢеҖ’иІЁпјҢгҖҢжҠ•дҝЎжә–еӮҷжҙ—дёҖиј©еӯҗзҡ„зў—гҖҚпјҢйЎҜзӨәеёӮе ҙе°ҚеҫҢзәҢиө°еӢўд»Қе……ж»ҝеҲҶжӯ§гҖӮ

пјҲе°ҒйқўзӨәж„Ҹең–/AIз”ҹжҲҗпјү

news.Title: SK Hynix Has a Massive Warning for Sandisk Stock Investors
news.Content: Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) stock has made investors significantly richer over the past year, delivering stunning gains of more than 3,660% as of this writing. The stock's phenomenal jump can be justified by the incredible increase in its revenue and earnings, fueled by the artificial intelligence (AI)-driven demand for storage chips. The demand for ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Sandisk (SNDK) has experienced significant gains (over 3,660% in the past year) driven by AI-fueled demand for NAND flash chips, leading to supply shortages and price increases.
вҖў SK Hynix plans to invest $51 billion in a new NAND flash production facility by 2029 to address the supply shortage, potentially impacting Sandisk's market share (SK Hynix has 18%, Sandisk has 13%).
вҖў Despite SK Hynix's expansion, the demand for AI data center storage is expected to keep the market undersupplied through 2028, suggesting Sandisk's growth momentum may continue.
вҖў Industry revenue for NAND flash is projected to increase significantly, with estimates reaching $379 billion by 2027, indicating sustained demand.
вҖў Sandisk trades at an attractive 27 times forward earnings, making it a potential buy despite its recent surge.
news.Keywords: NAND flash, AI demand, supply shortage, memory chips, SK Hynix

news.Title: SpaceX Joins the Nasdaq-100 Today. History Says the Stock Will Do This Next.
news.Content: Space Exploration Technologies (NASDAQ: SPCX) held its historic initial public offering (IPO) on June 12. The rocket company went public with a record market value of $1.7 trillion at its IPO price of $135 per share. SpaceX will be added to the Nasdaq-100 before the market opens on July 7. Its inclusion is unprecedented because the index has traditionally considered only stocks that ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў SpaceX (SPCX) is joining the Nasdaq-100 with a record IPO market value of $1.7 trillion, becoming eligible due to rule changes allowing faster entry for large IPOs.
вҖў Historically, stocks added to the Nasdaq-100 have seen average returns of 10% in six months and 18% in 12 months post-inclusion.
вҖў SpaceX's valuation is extremely high at 110 times sales, driven by its Starlink business (serving over 10 million subscribers) and potential AI infrastructure revenue.
вҖў Future revenue streams from AI products are uncertain, making the current valuation difficult to justify compared to peers like Rocket Lab.
вҖў The article mentions a 'Double Down' signal flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia, suggesting potential investment opportunities.
news.Keywords: IPO, Nasdaq-100, index inclusion, Starlink, AI infrastructure

news.Title: Is Apple Stock a Buy After Its Recent Pullback? Here's What History Suggests.
news.Content: Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) lost roughly $500 billion of its market cap over 25 days in June. That's the equivalent of an AbbVie (NYSE: ABBV), Caterpillar (NYSE: CAT), or Mastercard (NYSE: MA) being completely wiped out. What was once a solid year-to-date performance for Apple has turned into a puny gain. But is the stock a buy after its recent pullback? Here's what history suggests. Missed Nvidia in 2009? ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Apple (AAPL) experienced a market cap loss of approximately $500 billion in June, turning a solid year-to-date performance into a modest gain.
вҖў Concerns about Apple's new Siri AI assistant and recent price increases for Mac and iPad products due to memory and storage shortages contributed to the decline.
вҖў Historically, Apple stock has presented buying opportunities after pullbacks of 10% or more, with past recoveries driven by new product launches and services growth.
вҖў Despite current headwinds like memory cost increases (termed a 'hundred-year flood' by CEO Tim Cook), Apple's competitive advantages and loyal customer base remain intact.
вҖў Wall Street sentiment is generally bullish, with 62% of analysts rating Apple as a 'buy' or 'strong buy', and a consensus 12-month price target indicating potential upside.
news.Keywords: stock pullback, market cap, AI assistant, supply chain, analyst sentiment

news.Title: дёүжҳҹиӯүеҲёзңӢеҘҪйҹ“иӮЎй•·з·ҡжҪӣеҠӣ е»әиӯ°е–„з”ЁзҜҖзЁ…еёіжҲ¶еёғеұҖиіҮз”ў
news.Content:
е•ҶеӮіеӘ’ пҪңеҗіжүҝеІіпјҸеҸ°еҢ—е ұе°Һ

е„ҳз®Ўе…ЁзҗғйҮ‘иһҚеёӮе ҙжіўеӢ•еҠ еҠҮпјҢйҹ“еңӢиӮЎеёӮй•·жңҹжҠ•иіҮеғ№еҖјдҫқз„¶еҸ—еҲ°еёӮе ҙзңӢеҘҪгҖӮдёүжҳҹиӯүеҲёпјҲSamsung Securitiesпјүиҝ‘ж—ҘиЎЁзӨәпјҢе°Қж–ји‘—зңјй•·жңҹе ұй…¬зҡ„жҠ•иіҮдәәиҖҢиЁҖпјҢйҹ“иӮЎд»Қе…·еӮҷеҹәжң¬йқўе„ӘеӢўпјҢе»әиӯ°йҖҸйҒҺж”ҝеәңжҸҗдҫӣзҡ„зҜҖзЁ…еёіжҲ¶пјҢжҺЎеҸ–е®ҡжңҹгҖҒй•·жңҹзҡ„жҠ•иіҮзӯ–з•ҘпјҢз©©еҒҘзҙҜз©ҚиіҮз”ўгҖӮж №ж“ҡйҹ“еңӢгҖҠжҜҸж—Ҙз¶“жҝҹгҖӢе ұе°ҺпјҢдёүжҳҹиӯүеҲёй »йҒ“и§Јжұәж–№жЎҲзӯ–з•ҘеңҳйҡҠдё»з®ЎжҢҮеҮәпјҢеёӮе ҙзҹӯжңҹйңҮзӣӘеұ¬ж–јжҠ•иіҮйҒҺзЁӢдёӯзҡ„жӯЈеёёзҸҫиұЎпјҢдёҚжҮүеӣ жӯӨж”№и®Ҡй•·жңҹжҠ•иіҮиҰҸеҠғгҖӮе°Қжңүж„ҸеёғеұҖйҹ“еңӢеёӮе ҙзҡ„жҠ•иіҮдәәдҫҶиӘӘпјҢе–„з”ЁзҜҖзЁ…еёіжҲ¶дёҚеғ…еҸҜйҷҚдҪҺжҠ•иіҮжҲҗжң¬пјҢд№ҹжңүеҠ©ж–јжҸҗеҚҮй•·жңҹе ұй…¬зҺҮгҖӮжүҖи¬ӮзҜҖзЁ…еёіжҲ¶пјҢжҳҜж”ҝеәңзӮәйј“еӢөж°‘зңҫжҠ•иіҮжҲ–е„Іи“„жүҖиЁӯз«Ӣзҡ„е„Әжғ еҲ¶еәҰпјҢжҠ•иіҮж”¶зӣҠеҸҜдә«жңүе…ҚзЁ…гҖҒжёӣзЁ…жҲ–е»¶еҫҢиӘІзЁ…зӯүе„Әжғ пјҢи®“иіҮз”ўеңЁиӨҮеҲ©ж•ҲжһңдёӢжҢҒзәҢеўһеҖјгҖӮдёүжҳҹиӯүеҲёиӘҚзӮәпјҢеңЁе…ЁзҗғиіҮйҮ‘жҢҒзәҢй—ңжіЁдәәе·Ҙжҷәж…§пјҲAIпјүгҖҒеҚҠе°Һй«”еҸҠй«ҳз§‘жҠҖз”ўжҘӯзҡ„и¶ЁеӢўдёӢпјҢйҹ“еңӢдјҒжҘӯд»Қе…·еӮҷиүҜеҘҪзҡ„жҲҗй•·еӢ•иғҪпјҢеӣ жӯӨе°Қйҹ“иӮЎеҫҢеёӮз¶ӯжҢҒжӯЈеҗ‘зңӢжі•гҖӮе°Қж–јжңүж„ҸеёғеұҖжө·еӨ–еёӮе ҙзҡ„еҸ°зҒЈжҠ•иіҮдәәиҖҢиЁҖпјҢйҷӨдәҶй—ңжіЁеёӮе ҙжіўеӢ•пјҢд№ҹеҸҜеҫһй•·жңҹжҠ•иіҮгҖҒиіҮз”ўй…ҚзҪ®еҸҠзЁ…еӢҷиҰҸеҠғзӯүи§’еәҰжҖқиҖғпјҢе»әз«Ӣжӣҙз©©еҒҘзҡ„жҠ•иіҮзӯ–з•ҘгҖӮ

гҖҗжҸҗйҶ’жӮЁгҖ‘жҠ•иіҮжңүиіәжңүиі пјҢжң¬ж–Үеғ…дҫӣеҸғиҖғпјҢдёҚж§ӢжҲҗжҠ•иіҮе»әиӯ°гҖӮ

news.Title: еҸ°з©Қйӣ»зҸҫеңЁиғҪиІ·йҖІпјҹе°Ҳе®¶жӣқ3еҘҲзұігҖҒ2еҘҲзұіеӨ§еҲ©еӨҡ зӨәиӯҰйҖҷдәӢи—Ҹи®Ҡж•ё
news.Content:
еҸ°иӮЎд»ҠпјҲ7пјүж—ҘзөӮе ҙйҮҚжҢ«йҖҫеҚғй»һпјҢдёҖиҲүеӨұе®Ҳ46KеӨ§й—ңпјҢж¬ҠзҺӢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»е°ҫзӣӨзҝ»й»‘пјҢдёӢи·Ң20е…ғпјҢж”¶еңЁ2440е…ғпјҢйҡЁи‘—иӮЎеғ№йңҮзӣӘпјҢеёӮе ҙд№ҹжҢҒзәҢй—ңжіЁеҸ°з©Қйӣ»жҳҜеҗҰд»Қе…·еӮҷй•·жңҹжҠ•иіҮеғ№еҖјгҖӮгҖҠThe Motley FoolгҖӢеҲҶжһҗеё«Rick OrfordжҢҮеҮәпјҢеҸ—жғ ж–је…ҲйҖІиЈҪзЁӢеғ№ж јиӘҝжјІпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»жңӘдҫҶзҚІеҲ©жҲҗй•·еӢ•иғҪд»Қзӣёз•¶еј·еӢҒпјҢдёҚйҒҺпјҢиӮЎеғ№е·ІзҙҜз©ҚдёҚе°ҸжјІе№…пјҢеҠ дёҠзҫҺеңӢе°ҲеҲ©иЁҙиЁҹд»ҚеӯҳеңЁдёҚзўәе®ҡжҖ§зҡ„жғ…жіҒдёӢпјҢжҠ•иіҮдәәд»Қй ҲеҜ©ж…Һи©•дј°йўЁйҡӘгҖӮ

Rick OrfordиЎЁзӨәпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»зӣ®еүҚд»Қз©©еұ…е…Ёзҗғжҷ¶ең“д»Је·ҘйҫҚй ӯпјҢзӮәиҳӢжһңгҖҒијқйҒ”гҖҒи¶…еҫ®гҖҒеҚҡйҖҡеҸҠй«ҳйҖҡзӯүз§‘жҠҖеӨ§е» з”ҹз”ўе…ҲйҖІжҷ¶зүҮгҖӮжҲӘиҮі2026е№ҙз¬¬дёҖеӯЈпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»е…Ёзҗғжҷ¶ең“д»Је·ҘеёӮеҚ зҺҮе·ІжҺҘиҝ‘72%пјҢжҢҒзәҢеӨ§е№…й ҳе…Ҳз«¶зҲӯе°ҚжүӢдёүжҳҹйӣ»еӯҗгҖӮйҷӨдәҶ3еҘҲзұіиЈҪзЁӢе·Із©©е®ҡйҮҸз”ўпјҢ2еҘҲзұіиЈҪзЁӢд№ҹжӯЈејҸжҠ•з”ўпјҢеҚҮзҙҡзүҲN2PиЈҪзЁӢй җиЁҲе°Үж–јд»Ҡе№ҙдёӢеҚҠе№ҙжҺЁеҮәпјҢеҸҜжңӣжҢҒзәҢж»ҝи¶іAIгҖҒй«ҳж•ҲиғҪйҒӢз®—пјҲHPCпјүеҸҠжҷәж…§еһӢжүӢж©ҹзӯүй«ҳйҡҺжҷ¶зүҮйңҖжұӮгҖӮ

Rick OrfordиӘҚзӮәпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»жңӘдҫҶжңҖеӨ§зҡ„жҲҗй•·еҘ‘ж©ҹпјҢеңЁж–јжҢҒзәҢжҸҗеҚҮе…ҲйҖІиЈҪзЁӢзҡ„е®ҡеғ№иғҪеҠӣпјҢд»Ҡе№ҙ3еҘҲзұіжҷ¶ең“д»Је·Ҙеғ№ж јжңҖй«ҳиӘҝжјІзҙ„15%пјҢ2еҘҲзұіжҷ¶ең“жҜҸзүҮе ұеғ№жӣҙжҺҘиҝ‘3иҗ¬зҫҺе…ғпјҢеүөдёӢжӯ·еҸІж–°й«ҳгҖӮ

зҚІеҲ©ж–°еј•ж“Һе•ҹеӢ•

з”ұж–јеҸ°з©Қйӣ»е…ҲйҖІиЈҪзЁӢз”ўиғҪе№ҫд№Һж»ҝијүгҖҒе®ўжҲ¶йңҖжұӮдҫқиҲҠеј·еӢҒпјҢеғ№ж јиӘҝеҚҮдёҰжңӘеҪұйҹҝжҺҘе–®пјҢеҸҚиҖҢеҸҜжңӣеё¶еӢ•жҜӣеҲ©зҺҮиҲҮзҚІеҲ©еҗҢжӯҘжҸҗеҚҮгҖӮеёӮе ҙй җдј°пјҢиӢҘе…ҲйҖІиЈҪзЁӢе…ЁйқўеҸҚжҳ ж–°еғ№ж јпјҢеҸҜжңӣзӮәеҸ°з©Қйӣ»еўһеҠ ж•ёеҚҒе„„зҫҺе…ғзҮҹж”¶пјҢдёҰжҺЁеҚҮжҜҸиӮЎзӣҲйӨҳпјҲEPSпјүзҙ„10%иҮі15%гҖӮ

з„¶иҖҢпјҢRick Orfordд№ҹжҸҗйҶ’пјҢжҠ•иіҮдәәд»ҚйңҖз•ҷж„ҸжҪӣеңЁйўЁйҡӘгҖӮйҷӨдәҶең°з·Јж”ҝжІ»еӣ зҙ еӨ–пјҢзҫҺеңӢе°ҲеҲ©иЁҙиЁҹд№ҹжҳҜеёӮе ҙй—ңжіЁз„Ұй»һгҖӮиӢҘжңҖзөӮиЈҒжұәдёҚеҲ©пјҢзҗҶи«–дёҠеҸҜиғҪеҪұйҹҝйғЁеҲҶе…ҲйҖІжҷ¶зүҮеңЁзҫҺеңӢеёӮе ҙзҡ„йҠ·е”®гҖӮ

зҫҺеңӢе°ҲеҲ©жҲ°жҒҗжҲҗи®Ҡж•ё

ж №ж“ҡгҖҠAxiosгҖӢж—ҘеүҚе ұе°ҺпјҢж„ӣзҲҫиҳӯе°ҲеҲ©жҺҲж¬Ҡе…¬еҸёLongitude LicensingиҲҮMarlin Semiconductorе·Іе…ұеҗҢеҗ‘зҫҺеңӢеңӢйҡӣиІҝжҳ“е§”е“ЎжңғпјҲITCпјүжҸҗеҮәз”іиЁҙпјҢжҢҮжҺ§еҸ°з©Қйӣ»еҲ©з”Ёе…ҲйҖІиЈҪзЁӢз”ҹз”ўзҡ„жҷ¶зүҮдҫөзҠҜе…¶е°ҲеҲ©ж¬ҠпјҢж¶үеҸҠAIеҠ йҖҹеҷЁжҷ¶зүҮзҡ„й—ңйҚөжҠҖиЎ“гҖӮе°ҚжӯӨпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»еӣһжҮүпјҢе…¬еҸёеңЁе…ЁзҗғжүҖжңүзҮҹйҒӢж“ҡй»һеқҮйҒөеҫӘзӣёй—ңжі•еҫӢиҰҸзҜ„гҖӮ

з¶“жҝҹйғЁжҷәж…§иІЎз”ўеұҖй•·е»–жүҝеЁҒеүҮиЎЁзӨәпјҢжҸҗе‘ҠеҸ°з©Қйӣ»зҡ„е…©е®¶е…¬еҸёеұ¬ж–јжҘӯз•Ңдҝ—зЁұзҡ„гҖҢе°ҲеҲ©иҹ‘иһӮгҖҚгҖӮд»–жҢҮеҮәпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»йҒҺеҺ»еңЁе°ҲеҲ©жӘўзҙўеҸҠдҫөж¬ҠиЁҙиЁҹж–№йқўз¶“й©—иұҗеҜҢпјҢдё”еӢқиЁҙзҙҖйҢ„иүҜеҘҪпјҢе°Қе…¶иҷ•зҗҶиғҪеҠӣж·ұе…·дҝЎеҝғпјӣзӣ®еүҚеҸ°з©Қйӣ»д№ҹе·Іеҗ‘зҫҺеңӢе°ҲеҲ©е•ҶжЁҷеұҖпјҲUSPTOпјүжҸҗеҮәе°ҲеҲ©з„Ўж•ҲжҢ‘жҲ°пјҢеёҢжңӣи—үжӯӨеҢ–и§Јзӣёй—ңзҲӯиӯ°гҖӮ

еёӮе ҙеҲҶжһҗжҷ®йҒҚд№ҹиӘҚзӮәпјҢжӯӨйЎһе°ҲеҲ©иЁҙиЁҹеӨҡеҚҠжңҖзөӮд»Ҙе’Ңи§Јж”¶е ҙпјҢе…ЁйқўзҰҒжӯўзӣёй—ңжҷ¶зүҮйҖІеҸЈзҫҺеңӢзҡ„еҸҜиғҪжҖ§дёҰдёҚй«ҳгҖӮ

news.Title: Nigeria to investigate tech firms over news content use
news.Content: By Tife Owolabi LAGOS, July 7 (Reuters) - Nigerian President Bola Tinubu has directed the country's competition regulator to investigate major technology companies вҖҢover alleged anti-competitive practices and unauthorised use of news content, ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Nigeria's President has ordered an investigation into major tech firms, including Meta and Alphabet, for alleged anti-competitive practices and unauthorized use of news content.
вҖў The inquiry, led by the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (FCCPC), will examine market dominance, use of copyrighted content for AI training, and monetization of journalistic material.
вҖў This investigation could set a precedent for regulating global digital platforms in Africa, similar to actions taken in South Africa, France, Australia, and Canada.
вҖў Regulators worldwide are examining whether tech companies should compensate publishers for content used to train AI or generate revenue.
вҖў Companies like Meta, Alphabet, and X did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
news.Keywords: tech regulation, news content, anti-competitive practices, AI training data, digital platforms

news.Title: еҸ°иӮЎи·ҢеҚғй»һиғҪжҺҘеҲҖпјҹ е°Ҳе®¶е–ҠгҖҢ53000жІ’е•ҸйЎҢгҖҚпјҡжҠ„еә•иІ·й»һеңЁйҖҷеӨ©
news.Content:
еӨ§зӣӨзӢӮзҖү1077й»һи·Ңз ҙжңҲз·ҡеј•зҷјеёӮе ҙжҒҗж…ҢпјҢеҲҶжһҗеё«е‘Ёд»ЈйҒӢеңЁиҮүжӣёзҷјж–ҮиЎЁзӨәпјҢеј·иӘҝйҖҷдёҰйқһеҲ©з©әдҫҶиҘІпјҢиҖҢжҳҜе–®зҙ”гҖҢжјІеӨҡдәҶгҖҚзҡ„еҒҘеә·еӣһжӘ”пјҢдёҰйҮҚз”іеӨ§зӣӨжіўж®өзӣ®жЁҷдёҠзңӢ53000й»һзҡ„й җжё¬е®Ңе…ЁжІ’жңүе•ҸйЎҢгҖӮд»–жҢҮеҮәпјҢзӣ®еүҚйҮҸиғҪиҗҺзё®дёҰйқһж”ҫйҮҸйҮҚж®әпјҢдё»иҰҒжҳҜжҙ—ж·ЁиҝҪй«ҳжө®йЎҚпјҢй җиЁҲзҹӯз·ҡйӮ„йңҖеЈ“дҪҺж•ҙзҗҶдёҖеӨ©пјҢеҫ…еӣһжё¬10ж—Ҙз·ҡдё”йҮҸзё®жҷӮпјҢжүҚжҳҜжЁҷжә–зҡ„жҠ„еә•жҷӮж©ҹгҖӮ

еҸ°иӮЎеӨ§и·ҢйқһеҲ©з©әе–®зҙ”жјІеӨҡдәҶ

еҸ°иӮЎд»ҠеӨ©зӮәд»Җйәјжңғи·ҢпјҹеүҚеңӢйҡӣеӨ§йҖҡжҠ•йЎ§з ”з©¶йғЁеүҜзёҪе‘Ёд»ЈйҒӢиЎЁзӨәпјҢж №жң¬дёҚжҳҜд»ҖйәјеҲ©з©әпјҢе°ұжҳҜе–®зҙ”жјІеӨҡдәҶгҖӮжҢҮж•ёйҖЈжӢүдёҖе‘ЁпјҢйӣў5ж—Ҙз·ҡд№–йӣўжӢүеҲ°еҝ«2%пјҢеүҚй«ҳйҷ„иҝ‘жң¬дҫҶе°ұеҚЎдәҶдёҖе ҶеҘ—зүўи§ЈеҘ—е–®пјҢеӨ–иіҮй ҶжүӢеңЁжңҹиІЁз«ҜеҠ й»һз©әе–®иӘҝзҜҖпјҢжҠ•дҝЎй ҶеӢўеҒҡеҖӢеғ№е·®жҙ—жө®йЎҚгҖӮйҮҸиғҪжҳҜзё®зҡ„пјҢдёҚжҳҜж”ҫйҮҸйҮҚж®әгҖӮйҖҷе°ұеҸ«еҒҘеә·еӣһжӘ”пјҢжҠҠиҝҪй«ҳзҡ„йҹӯиҸңжҙ—д№ҫж·ЁпјҢеҫҢйқўжӢүиө·дҫҶжүҚиј•й¬ҶгҖӮ

дёүйқўж”Ҝж’җ53000й»һйқһз©әи©ұ

е‘Ёд»ЈйҒӢиЎЁзӨәпјҢеҫҲеӨҡдәәиіӘз–‘53000жҳҜдёҚжҳҜе–Ҡз©әи©ұгҖӮеқҰзҷҪи¬ӣпјҢеҰӮжһңжҳҜ10иҗ¬й»һпјҢжҲ‘иҰәеҫ—йӮ„жңүй»һеӨӘиҷӣпјҢдҪҶжҳҜпјҢ53000й»һеҹәжң¬дёҠжІ’жңүе•ҸйЎҢгҖӮ

еҹәжң¬йқўпјҡ AIдјәжңҚеҷЁиЁӮе–®жҺ’еҲ°2027е№ҙеә•пјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»3nmгҖҒ2nmз”ўиғҪе…Ёж»ҝпјҢж•ҙеҖӢеҚҠе°Һй«”дҫӣжҮүйҸҲз¬¬дёүеӯЈзҚІеҲ©йӮ„иҰҒеҶҚдҝ®й«ҳгҖӮд»Ҡе№ҙдёҠеёӮж«ғж•ҙй«”EPSжҲҗй•·зҺҮеёӮе ҙе…ұиӯҳ25%иө·и·іпјҢзҸҫеңЁжң¬зӣҠжҜ”йӣўжӯ·еҸІй«ҳй»һйӮ„жңүдёҖеӨ§ж®өпјҢж №жң¬жІ’еҲ°жіЎжІ«зҡ„зЁӢеәҰгҖӮ

жҠҖиЎ“йқўпјҡ йҖҷжіўжҳҜе№ҙеҲқе•ҹеӢ•зҡ„дё»еҚҮж®өпјҢдёҚи«–жҳҜжіўжөӘжё¬е№…йӮ„жҳҜеһӢж…Ӣж»ҝи¶ій»һпјҢ53000йғҪжҳҜжңҖеҹәжң¬зҡ„жіўж®өзӣ®жЁҷгҖӮеӯЈз·ҡд»°и§’жІ’и®ҠпјҢеӨҡй ӯи¶ЁеӢўе°ұжІ’з ҙгҖӮ

зұҢзўјйқўпјҡ жӣҙдёҚз”ЁжҖ•пјҢеӨ–иіҮйҖҷжіўжҳҜзҹӯз·ҡзҚІеҲ©дәҶзөҗпјҢдёҚжҳҜеҸҚжүӢдҪҲз©әгҖӮжң¬еңҹеЈҪйҡӘгҖҒжҠ•дҝЎзҡ„иіҮйҮ‘йӮ„еңЁж…ўж…ўйҖІе ҙпјҢжүҝжҺҘеҠӣйҒ“еӨ пјҢи·ҢдёҚж·ұгҖӮ

е…©еӨ©еҫҢзңӢжә–2еӨ§жҢҮжЁҷжҠ„еә•

еҸ°иӮЎзҹӯз·ҡжҖҺйәјиө°пјҹе‘Ёд»ЈйҒӢеҲҶжһҗпјҢд»ҠеӨ©еҸӘжҳҜз¬¬дёҖеӨ©еӣһжӘ”пјҢжҳҺеӨ©йӮ„жңүдёҖеӨ©иҰҒеЈ“гҖӮдёҚиҰҒжҖҘи‘—зҸҫеңЁиЎқйҖІеҺ»жҺҘйЈӣеҲҖпјҢд№ҹеҲҘеҡҮеҲ°дәӮз ҚеңЁжңҖдҪҺй»һпјҢжӣҙеҲҘжүӢзҷўжӯҗеҚ°жҺҘеҚҠеұұи…°гҖӮж—©дёҖеӨ©йҖІе ҙпјҢеҸҜиғҪеӨҡжүӣ3%пјҢе®Ңе…ЁжІ’еҝ…иҰҒгҖӮеӨ§жҰӮе…©еӨ©еҫҢпјҢжҢҮж•ёеӣһжё¬10ж—Ҙз·ҡйҷ„иҝ‘гҖҒйҮҸзё®еҲ°д»ҠеӨ©зҡ„8жҲҗе·ҰеҸіпјҢе°ұжҳҜжЁҷжә–зҡ„жҠ„еә•иЁҠиҷҹгҖӮ

йҺ–е®ҡдёүжӘ”AIж ёеҝғдё»з·ҡ

е‘Ёд»ЈйҒӢе»әиӯ°дёүжӘ”еҸҜд»Ҙз·ҠзӣҜеӣһжӘ”зҡ„жЁҷзҡ„пјҢйғҪжҳҜйҖҷжіўAIдё»з·ҡзҡ„ж ёеҝғпјҢжӢүеӣһе°ұжҳҜж’ҝдҫҝе®ңзҡ„еҘҪж©ҹжңғгҖӮ

пјҲе°ҒйқўзӨәж„Ҹең–/AIз”ҹжҲҗпјү

news.Title: гҖҲйҷёиӮЎзӣӨеҫҢгҖүгҖҢе·қзҝ’жңғгҖҚеҸҜжңӣ9жңҲеә•зҷ»е ҙ еёӮе ҙжңӘй ҳжғ… дёүеӨ§жҢҮж•ёиө°дҪҺ
news.Content:
дёӯеңӢ A иӮЎдёүеӨ§жҢҮж•ё 7 ж—ҘпјҲе‘ЁдәҢпјүиө°дҪҺпјҢзҫҺеңӢзёҪзөұе·қжҷ® 6 ж—ҘиЎЁзӨәпјҢй җе®ҡ 9 жңҲ 24 ж—ҘеүҚеҫҢеңЁзҫҺеңӢиҲҮдёӯеңӢеңӢе®¶дё»еёӯзҝ’иҝ‘е№іиҰӢйқўпјҢдҪҶеёӮе ҙдёҰжңӘй ҳжғ…гҖӮ

дёҠиӯүжҢҮж•ё 7 ж—Ҙж”¶е ұ 3,990.24 й»һпјҢи·Ң 1.26%гҖӮж·ұиӯүжҲҗжҢҮж”¶е ұ 15,225.11 й»һпјҢи·Ң 1.24%гҖӮеүөжҘӯжқҝжҢҮж”¶е ұ 3,911.91 й»һпјҢи·Ң 0.94%гҖӮ

е·қжҷ®еңЁзҷҪе®®жҙ»еӢ•дёӯи«ҮеҸҠиҲҲе»әеӨ§еһӢе®ҙжңғе»іиЁҲз•«жҷӮпјҢжҸҗеҲ°зҝ’иҝ‘е№іеҸҜиғҪдҫҶиЁӘзҡ„е®үжҺ’гҖӮд»–иЎЁзӨәпјҢзҷҪе®®йңҖиҰҒдёҖй–“жӣҙеӨ§зҡ„е®ҙжңғе»іпјҢд»ҘдҫҝжҺҘеҫ…зҝ’иҝ‘е№ізӯүеӨ–еңӢе…ғйҰ–гҖӮ

е·қжҷ®иӘӘпјҡгҖҢдҫӢеҰӮпјҢзҝ’иҝ‘е№ідё»еёӯе°ҮеңЁ 9 жңҲеә•е·ҰеҸідҫҶеҲ°йҖҷиЈЎпјҢжҲ‘зӣёдҝЎжҳҜ 9 жңҲ 24 ж—ҘеүҚеҫҢгҖӮжҲ‘еҖ‘йңҖиҰҒдёҖй–“еӨ§еһӢе®ҙжңғе»іпјҢжүҚиғҪе®№зҙҚж•ёеҚғдәәпјҢжҜҸеҖӢдәәйғҪжғіиҰӢд»–гҖӮгҖҚ

иӢҘдҫқе·қжҷ®йҖҸйңІзҡ„жҷӮй–“иҰҸеҠғпјҢзҝ’иҝ‘е№іиЁӘзҫҺиЎҢзЁӢе°ҮиҲҮдёҖе№ҙдёҖеәҰзҡ„иҒҜеҗҲеңӢеӨ§жңғй«ҳеұӨе‘Ёзӣёдә’йҠңжҺҘгҖӮе·қжҷ®й җиЁҲж–ј 9 жңҲ 22 ж—ҘеңЁиҒҜеҗҲеңӢеӨ§жңғзҷјиЎЁжј”иӘӘпјҢдёҰдҫқж…ЈдҫӢеңЁзҙҗзҙ„еҒңз•ҷж•ёж—ҘпјҢиҲҮеӨҡеңӢй ҳиў–иҲүиЎҢйӣҷйӮҠжңғи«ҮгҖӮзҝ’иҝ‘е№іиҮӘ 2012 е№ҙдёҠд»»д»ҘдҫҶпјҢеғ…еҮәеёӯйҒҺдёҖж¬ЎиҒҜеҗҲеңӢеӨ§жңғгҖӮ

е·қжҷ®е…ҲеүҚжӣҫиЎЁзӨәпјҢеёҢжңӣж–ј 9 жңҲжҺҘеҫ…зҝ’иҝ‘е№іиЁӘзҫҺпјҢжӯӨж¬ЎжҳҜйҰ–еәҰжҸҗеҮәијғжҳҺзўәжҷӮй–“й»һгҖӮдёҚйҒҺпјҢзӣ®еүҚзҷҪе®®иҲҮдёӯеңӢе®ҳж–№е°ҡжңӘжӯЈејҸе…¬еёғиЁӘе•Ҹе®үжҺ’гҖӮ

е·қжҷ®иҲҮзҝ’иҝ‘е№ід»Ҡе№ҙ 5 жңҲжӣҫеңЁеҢ—дә¬иҲүиЎҢеӮҷеҸ—зҹҡзӣ®зҡ„й«ҳеі°жңғпјҢйӣҷж–№е°ӢжұӮеңЁдјҠжң—жҲ°дәӢеј•зҷјз·ҠејөеұҖеӢўеҫҢпјҢйҖІдёҖжӯҘз©©е®ҡе…©еңӢй—ңдҝӮгҖӮ

е„ҳз®ЎзҫҺдёӯиҝ‘жңҹжҢҒзәҢж”№е–„йӣҷйӮҠдә’еӢ•пјҢдёҰеңЁз¶“иІҝй ҳеҹҹеҸ–еҫ—йғЁеҲҶйҖІеұ•пјҢдҪҶйӣҷж–№д»ҚйқўиҮЁеӨҡй …ж•Ҹж„ҹиӯ°йЎҢпјҢеҢ…жӢ¬дёӯжқұеұҖеӢўгҖҒеҸ°зҒЈе•ҸйЎҢпјҢд»ҘеҸҠе·қжҷ®жҢҒзәҢжҺЁеӢ•зҡ„й—ңзЁ…ж”ҝзӯ–гҖӮ

news.Title: еҸ°иӮЎ2еӨ§еҺҹеӣ зӘҒйҮҚжҢ«еҚғй»һпјҒзҹӯз·ҡжІ’з«ҷеӣһгҖҢеЈ“еҠӣй»һдҪҚгҖҚе°ұж…ҳдәҶ
news.Content:
еҸ°иӮЎд»ҠпјҲ7ж—ҘпјүеҠҮзғҲйңҮзӣӘпјҢж—©зӣӨдёҖеәҰдёҠжјІи¶…йҒҺ400й»һпјҢдҪҶеҚҲзӣӨиіЈеЈ“жІүйҮҚпјҢзҝ»й»‘дёӢж®әеҫҢйҮҚжҢ«и¶…йҒҺеҚғй»һпјҢи®“дёҚе°‘жҠ•иіҮдәәзӣёз•¶еӣ°жғ‘гҖӮжі•дәәиӘҚзӮәпјҢйҷӨдәҶеҸ—еҲ°дәһиӮЎиө°ејұпјҢеёӮе ҙиҝҪеғ№дҝЎеҝғдёҚи¶іпјҢеҪўжҲҗеҺ»ж§“жЎҝгҖҢеӨҡж®әеӨҡгҖҚзҸҫиұЎпјҢзҹӯжңҹе…§д»Қеҝ…й Ҳи§ҖеҜҹж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎеӢ•еҗ‘пјҢеӨ§зӣӨиғҪеҗҰеҝ«йҖҹз«ҷеӣһжңҲз·ҡе°ҮжҳҜй—ңйҚөгҖӮ

еҸ°иӮЎдёҖеҸЈж°ЈеӨұе®Ҳ46000й»һй—ңеҚЎпјҢ10ж—Ҙз·ҡзҙ„46057й»һеҸҠжңҲз·ҡ45710й»һпјҢжңҖдҪҺи§ёеҸҠ45432.02й»һпјҢж”¶зӣӨзӮә45479.11й»һпјҢй«ҳдҪҺйңҮзӣӘйҒ”1535й»һгҖӮеҸ°иӮЎзӣӨйқўз¶ жІ№жІ№пјҢйҖҫеҚғе®¶ж”¶и·ҢпјҢзҶұй–Җж—ҸзҫӨеҸҠж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎе…ЁйқўеӨұе®ҲпјҢеғ…еӨ§еһӢйҮ‘жҺ§иӮЎж’җзӣӨпјҢдәӨжҠ•жғ…з·’й©ҹи®ҠгҖӮ

жі•дәәиӘҚзӮәпјҢзҹӯжңҹйңҖиҰҒи§ҖеҜҹеҸ°иӮЎиғҪеҗҰеҝ«йҖҹз«ҷеӣһжңҲз·ҡпјҢиӢҘз„ЎеҠӣжӯўи·ҢжҒҗйқўиҮЁжӣҙй•·жңҹзҡ„зӣӨж•ҙпјҢз”ҡиҮіеҠҮзғҲдёӢжҺўзҡ„еЈ“еҠӣпјҢжӯӨжҷӮж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎзҡ„зӢҖжіҒе°ҮжҳҜйҮҚй»һгҖӮеҸ°з©Қйӣ»д»Ҡж—ҘдёҖи·ҜиӢҰж’җпјҢдҪҶе°ҫзӣӨзҝ»й»‘пјҢжңҖзөӮдёӢи·Ң20е…ғж”¶еңЁжңҖдҪҺзҡ„2440е…ғгҖӮ

ж°ёиұҗжңҹиІЁиӘҚзӮәпјҢжҖҘж®әйҖјеҮәиһҚиіҮжё…жҙ—еЈ“еҠӣпјҢеёӮе ҙжғ…з·’жҳҺйЎҜжңқз©әж–№еӮҫж–ңгҖӮжҒҗж…Ңжғ…з·’и”“е»¶дёӢпјҢзҹӯз·ҡзӣӨеӢўйҖІе…ҘзұҢзўјйҮҚзө„зҡ„жҷӮеҲ»гҖӮжӯӨжҷӮиҲҮе…¶йҒҺж—©зҢңжё¬еә•йғЁгҖҒзӣІзӣ®жүҝжҺҘеј·еӢўиӮЎзҡ„жӢүеӣһдҝ®жӯЈпјҢжҠ•иіҮдәәжҮүйҖҖе ҙи§ҖжңӣпјҢйқңеҫ…дё»жөҒй«ҳеғ№иӮЎжӯўи·ҢеҸҠзұҢзўјжІүжҫұгҖӮ

дёүеӨ§жі•дәәеҗҲиЁҲиіЈи¶…946.91е„„е…ғпјҢеӨ–иіҮеҸҠйҷёиіҮиіЈи¶…547.31е„„е…ғпјҢиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶиіЈи¶…487.33е„„е…ғпјҢжҠ•дҝЎиІ·и¶…87.73е„„е…ғпјҢеӨ–иіҮжҢҒзәҢе°ҮиіҮйҮ‘иҪүеҫҖзҫҺе…ғиіҮз”ўпјҢж–°еҸ°е№Је…ҢзҫҺе…ғзӘҒз ҙ32е…ғй—ңеҚЎеҫҢпјҢд»Ҡж—ҘзәҢиІ¶йҖҫ1и§’гҖӮ

еӨ–иіҮдёҠе‘ЁиіЈи¶…1392.99е„„е…ғпјҢжң¬е‘ЁзәҢз«ҷиіЈж–№пјҢе…©еӨ©е·ІзӢӮз Қ802.35е„„е…ғпјҢи§ҖеҜҹжҳҜеҗҰжңүеҸ°з©Қйӣ»жі•иӘӘжңғиЎҢжғ…пјҢеҗҰеүҮиіҮйҮ‘еҮәзҸҫијӘеӢ•еҫҢжңғеӮҫеҗ‘дҪҺеҹәжңҹгҖҒйҳІзҰҰжҖ§еҸҠеҹәжң¬йқўиүҜеҘҪзҡ„ж—ҸзҫӨгҖӮ

news.Title: President Donald Trump Took a Direct Jab at the Fed, Yet Again -- and It's a Disaster in the Making for Wall Street
news.Content: From a purely statistical standpoint, the annualized returns of the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES: ^DJI), S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC), and Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX: ^IXIC) under President Donald Trump have been higher than most other presidents since the late 1890s. However, these gains haven't been earned without bouts of historic volatility and periods of heightened uncertainty. Missed ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў President Donald Trump has repeatedly criticized the Federal Reserve's monetary policy, advocating for significantly lower interest rates.
вҖў Trump's calls for lower rates are motivated by potential benefits like increased hiring, economic growth, and reduced national debt servicing costs.
вҖў Despite the Fed's independence, Trump's criticisms, particularly regarding interest rate decisions, could undermine investor confidence in the central bank.
вҖў The FOMC's potential decision to raise rates to combat inflation, driven partly by the AI data center build-out, is seen as negative for a historically pricey stock market.
вҖў The article suggests that Trump's consistent undermining of the Fed's credibility poses a greater risk to the stock market than potential rate hikes.
news.Keywords: Federal Reserve, interest rates, monetary policy, inflation, market confidence

news.Title: еӨ–иіҮеӨ§иҲүжҸҗж¬ҫгҖҒеҸ°иӮЎжҢ«и·ҢжӢ–зҙҜпјҢж–°еҸ°е№ЈйҖұдәҢйҮҚиІ¶1.18и§’йҖЈдә”и·Ң
news.Content:
гҖҗиІЎиЁҠеҝ«е ұпјҸеҠүж•ҸеӨ«гҖ‘еҸ°еҢ—еӨ–еҢҜеёӮе ҙйҖұдәҢж–°еҸ°е№Је…ҢзҫҺе…ғеҢҜеғ№иө°иІ¶гҖӮжҳҹе№ЈгҖҒжі°йҠ–йҷӨеӨ–дё»иҰҒдәһжҙІиІЁе№ЈйҖұдәҢжҷ®еҚҮпјҢйҖұдёҖж‘ңз ҙ32е…ғеғ№дҪҚзҡ„еҸ°е№ЈйҖЈдә”и·ҢгҖҒиЎЁзҸҫзӣёе°ҚиҗҪеҫҢпјҢиҲҮжӯӨеҗҢжҷӮпјҢеҸ°иӮЎйҖұдәҢиө°еӢўиёүи№ҢпјҢеҠ ж¬ҠжҢҮж•ёжҢ«и·Ң2.31%пјҢеӨ–иіҮиіЈи¶…дёҠеёӮиӮЎзҘЁ547.31е„„е…ғпјҢиҝ‘еӣӣж—ҘиіЈи¶…йҖҫ2,470е„„е…ғпјҢд№ҹд»ӨеҸ°е№ЈжүҝеЈ“гҖӮж–°еҸ°е№Је…ҢзҫҺе…ғеҢҜзҺҮйҖұдәҢд»Ҙ32.143е…ғдҪңж”¶пјҢиҲҮеүҚдёҖдәӨжҳ“ж—ҘжҜ”ијғпјҢеҸ°е№ЈеҢҜзҺҮеӨ§иІ¶1.18и§’гҖӮйҠҖиЎҢй–“ж–°еҸ°е№Је…ҢзҫҺе…ғжңҖй«ҳжҲҗдәӨеғ№зӮә32.030е…ғпјҢжңҖдҪҺжҲҗдәӨеғ№зӮә32.176е…ғпјҢжҲҗдәӨйҮҸзӮә19е„„4,600иҗ¬зҫҺе…ғгҖӮ

ж №ж“ҡе…©дҪҚеӨ–еҢҜдәӨжҳ“е“ЎиЎЁзӨәпјҢиҝ‘е№ҫж—ҘиҲҮеҸ°зҒЈеӨ®иЎҢеңЁжәқйҖҡеӨ§йЎҚзҫҺе…ғиіЈе–®жҷӮпјҢйҒҮеҲ°еӨ®иЎҢиҰҒжұӮз•¶еӨ©иіЈе®Ңзҡ„жғ…жіҒгҖӮдёҚйҒҺпјҢеӨ®иЎҢдёҰжңӘи§ЈйҮӢзӣёй—ңжҢҮеј•зҡ„еҺҹеӣ гҖӮйҖҷиҲҮд»ҘеҫҖеҸ°е№ЈеҚҮеӢўијғеӨ§жҷӮпјҢеӨ®иЎҢиҰҒжұӮеҲҶж•ЈеӨҡеӨ©иіЈеҮәз”ҡиҮій§Ғеӣһзҡ„жғ…жіҒдёҚеҗҢгҖӮ

жӯӨеӨ–пјҢеҸ°зҒЈеӨ®иЎҢеӨ–еҢҜеұҖеұҖй•·и”Ў烱ж°‘йҖұдёҖж–јиЁҳиҖ…жңғдёҠиЎЁзӨәпјҢ7жңҲеҸҠ8жңҲйҖІе…ҘиӮЎеҲ©зҷјж”ҫеӯЈзҜҖпјҢд»Ҡе№ҙзҡ„ж•ҙй«”иӮЎеҲ©йҮ‘йЎҚз•ҘеӨҡдёҖдәӣпјҢеӨ–иіҮжӢҝеҲ°еҫҢеҸҜиғҪеҢҜеҮәпјҢдҪҶд№ҹеҸҜиғҪз•ҷеңЁеҸ°иӮЎжҢҒзәҢжҠ•иіҮгҖӮ

9жңҲд»ҪиҒҜйӮҰе…¬й–ӢеёӮе ҙе§”е“Ўжңғ(FOMC)жңғиӯ°дёҚеӢ•зҡ„ж©ҹзҺҮйӮ„жҳҜжҜ”ијғеӨ§пјҢеӣ зӮәе•Ҷе“ҒйҖҡиҶЁе·Іжңүж”ҫз·©пјҢдё”зҫҺеңӢе°ұжҘӯеёӮе ҙжҜ”ијғз·©е’ҢпјҢиӢҘзҫҺе…ғиө°еӢўдёҚеҶҚйӮЈйәјеј·пјҢеёӮе ҙиіЈеҢҜж„ҸйЎҳжңғеӨ§дёҖй»һгҖӮ

news.Title: SpaceX Is Joining the Nasdaq-100 This Week. What This Means for Invesco QQQ Investors.
news.Content: Space Exploration Technologies (NASDAQ: SPCX), otherwise known as SpaceX, is joining the Nasdaq-100 index today. This means that exchange-traded funds (ETFs) tracking the index, including the Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ: QQQ), will soon own the stock indirectly. J.P. Morgan, part of JPMorgan Chase , expects this index inclusion to trigger about $4.3 billion in passive buying from index-tracking funds. Although this will serve as a clear near-term demand catalyst for SpaceX, Invesco QQQ Trust investors are also getting exposure to a founder-controlled company with a limited number of publicly traded shares (float) and an unprofitable business. Missed Nvidia in 2009? ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў SpaceX is joining the Nasdaq-100 index, which will lead to approximately $4.3 billion in passive buying from index-tracking funds like the Invesco QQQ Trust.
вҖў SpaceX's inclusion was expedited due to rule changes allowing large IPOs to join after 15 trading days, but its weight in the index may be limited by its small public float (3-4%).
вҖў Investors in the Invesco QQQ Trust gain exposure to SpaceX's space, satellite broadband (Starlink), and AI infrastructure businesses.
вҖў SpaceX reported a $4.94 billion net loss in 2025 and requires 12 months of public trading history and GAAP profitability for S&P 500 inclusion, which it currently does not meet.
вҖў Starlink, accounting for about 60% of SpaceX's $18.7 billion revenue in 2025, is its primary profit engine, generating $1.2 billion in operating profit for the connectivity segment in Q1.
news.Keywords: Nasdaq-100, index inclusion, passive buying, Starlink, public float

ж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎж–№йқўпјҢеӨ§еһӢиӮЎжҷ®йҒҚжүҝеЈ“гҖӮKOSPIжҢҮж•ёж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎеӨҡж•ёж”¶дҪҺпјҢSamsung ElectronicsгҖҒSK hynixгҖҒLG Energy Solution(373220.KS)гҖҒHyundai Motor(005380.KS)гҖҒSamsung C&T(028260.KS)гҖҒSamsung Life(032830.KS)гҖҒSamsung Electro-Mechanics(009150.KS)иҲҮSK Square(402340.KS)зӯүеқҮжүҝеҸ—иіЈеЈ“гҖӮзӣӨйқўйЎҜзӨәпјҢиіҮйҮ‘дёҰжңӘеӣ е–®дёҖдјҒжҘӯдә®зңјиІЎе ұиҖҢеӣһжөҒеӨ§еһӢиӮЎпјҢеҸҚиҖҢеңЁй«ҳжіўеӢ•дёӢеҠ йҖҹж’ӨеҮәгҖӮ

KOSDAQжҢҮж•ёж–№йқўпјҢи·Ңе№…зӣёе°Қијғе°ҸдҪҶд»ҚеҒҸејұпјҢзӣӨдёӯеҗҢжЁЈеҸҚиҰҶйңҮзӣӘпјҢж”¶зӣӨд»ҚиҗҪеңЁд»Ҡе№ҙдҪҺй»һйҷ„иҝ‘гҖӮеҖӢиӮЎиЎЁзҸҫеҲҶжӯ§пјҢAlteogen(196170.KS)гҖҒKolon TissueGene(950160.KS)гҖҒHLB(028300.KS)иҲҮABL Bio(298380.KS)ж”¶й«ҳпјҢEcoPro BM(247540.KS)гҖҒEcoPro(086520.KS)гҖҒRainbow Robotics(277810.KS)гҖҒWonik IPS(240810.KS)еүҮиө°дҪҺпјҢеҸҚжҳ дёӯе°ҸеһӢиӮЎд№ҹйӣЈд»Ҙж“әи„«ж•ҙй«”йўЁйҡӘеҒҸеҘҪйҷҚжә«зҡ„еҪұйҹҝгҖӮ

news.Title: 3 Millionaire-Maker Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks
news.Content: Artificial intelligence (AI) may be one of the best investing opportunities of our lifetimes. Several leading AI stocks have produced fivefold and tenfold returns over the past five years, but Sandisk's (NASDAQ: SNDK) nearly 4,000% gain over the past year highlights the possibilities. Investors who want to maximize their returns in this industry shouldn't focus on yesterday's winners. They should set their sights on smaller companies operating in ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Artificial intelligence (AI) presents a significant investment opportunity, with companies like Sandisk (SNDK) showing substantial past returns (nearly 4,000% in one year).
вҖў Cipher Digital, a neocloud provider, secures long-term deals for AI data center capacity, including a $5.5 billion deal with Amazon for 300 megawatts, and has a pipeline of over four gigawatts.
вҖў Silicon Motion Technology (SIMO), a NAND flash controller specialist, reported strong Q1 revenue of $342.1 million (23% sequential growth, over 100% year-over-year) and expects continued growth.
вҖў Sterling Infrastructure (STRL) is a construction company specializing in E-Infrastructure for AI data centers, with year-to-date share price doubling and 92% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1.
вҖў These companies are highlighted as 'millionaire-maker' AI stocks, focusing on smaller players gaining market share in the rapidly expanding AI sector.

news.Title: зөұдёҖжңҹиІЁпјҡйҹ“иӮЎеӣ ж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎеҲ©еӨҡеҮәзӣЎй–ӢдҪҺиө°дҪҺпјҢеҸ°иӮЎеҗҢжӯҘеҸ—еЈ“
news.Content: гҖҗиІЎиЁҠеҝ«е ұпјҸз·ЁијҜйғЁгҖ‘йҹ“еңӢж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎдёүжҳҹйӣ»еӯҗжё…жҷЁзҷјеёғиІЎе ұпјҢзҮҹжҘӯеҲ©зӣҠйҖЈдёүеӯЈеүөжӯ·еҸІе–®еӯЈж–°й«ҳгҖӮжғҹеј·еӢҒиІЎе ұзҷјеёғеҫҢпјҢиӮЎеғ№дёҚжјІеҸҚи·ҢпјҢйҮҚжҢ«7.7%гҖӮж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎиІЎе ұеҲ©еӨҡеҮәзӣЎпјҢдҪҝйҹ“еңӢиӮЎеёӮй–ӢдҪҺиө°дҪҺгҖӮеҸ°иӮЎеҸ—еҲ°йҹ“иӮЎжӢ–зҙҜеҪұйҹҝпјҢиЁҳжҶ¶й«”йЎһиӮЎжҷ®йҒҚиө°и·ҢпјҢдҪҝеҸ°жҢҮжңҹе»¶зәҢиҝ‘жңҹзӣӨж•ҙиЎҢжғ…гҖӮз¶ңи§Җжі•дәәзұҢзўјпјҢдёүеӨ§жі•дәәеҗҲиЁҲиіЈи¶…946.9е„„е…ғпјҢе…¶дёӯеӨ–иіҮиіЈи¶…547.3е„„е…ғпјҢиҖҢиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶиіЈи¶…487.3е„„е…ғпјҢжҠ•дҝЎеүҮиІ·и¶…87.7е„„е…ғгҖӮжӘўиҰ–йҒёж“Үж¬ҠйҮ‘жөҒпјҢеӨ–иіҮеёғеұҖ0.02е„„е…ғеҒҸеӨҡйғЁдҪҚпјҢиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶеүҮеёғеұҖ1.07е„„е…ғеҒҸеӨҡйғЁдҪҚгҖӮйҒёж“Үж¬ҠжңӘе№іеҖүйғЁеҲҶпјҢ7жңҲW2йҖұйҒёеғ№е№іиҝ‘9жӘ”иіЈж¬ҠжңҖеӨ§жңӘе№іеҖүдёӢйҷҚ1,500й»һиҮі45,500й»һпјҢиІ·ж¬ҠжңҖеӨ§жңӘе№іеҖүдёӢйҷҚ1,500й»һиҮі45,500й»һпјҢжңҖеӨ§OIеҚҖй–“дёӢз§»еҒҸз©әгҖӮжңҹиІЁж–№йқўпјҢеӨ–иіҮеӨңзӣӨжёӣзўј310еҸЈз©әе–®пјҢж—ҘзӣӨеүҮеҠ зўј609еҸЈз©әе–®пјҢжңӘе№іеҖүж·Ёз©әе–®еўһиҮі-79,177еҸЈпјӣеҚҒеӨ§зү№е®ҡжі•дәәжёӣзўј551еҸЈз©әе–®иҮі-7,481еҸЈгҖӮ
